name: Heatseeker Goal Blocker (Freeplay)
description:
# Heatseeker Goal Blocker

A BakkesMod plugin that allows you to block goals in Heatseeker mode, perfect for practicing redirects and maintaining rallies. Basically Heatseeker freeplay.

## Known Issues & Plas

- The main menu button when enabled overlays on top of the garage menu etc - will see if I can fix this in the future.. If it bothers you just turn off the main menu button in the plugin settings.
- I will style the main menu button eventually so it looks nicer :)

## Features

- Block either goal independently
- Enable/disable scoring on unblocked goals
- One-click launch for Heatseeker practice in settings menu and on main menu (LAN match, unlimited time/score (100 goal max as RL doesn't like me trying to do unlimited))
- Only Works in LAN matches or multiplayer using Rocket Plugin
- Heatseeker mode only
- Toggle goals on/off during gameplay
- Safe to use - won't affect online matches

## Installation

1) Install from Bakkesmod plugins site (recommended) - https://bakkesplugins.com/plugins/view/494

or

2) Download manually from Github releases and place in your plugins folder

## Usage

1. Click "Start Heatseeker Freeplay" on the main menu (top left, above Play)
   - OR Open BakkesMod (F2) -> Plugins -> Heatseeker Goal Blocker -> Launch Heatseeker Practice
2. Toggle which goal(s) you want to block
3. Optionally disable "Enable Scoring" to  prevent goals if a goal is unblocked for whatever reason

## Multiplayer Usage

For LAN matches with multiple players:
- Only the host needs to have rocket plugin installed
- All players in the match will see the goal blocking effect
- The host can toggle goals on/off during gameplay
- Other players don't need to install anything

## Source Code

Available on [GitHub](https://github.com/ncjsvr/HeatseekerGoalBlocker)

## Credits

Thanks to SoulDaMeep for help with the bCanBeScored memory flag.
Thanks to Acc70 for help with optimizations and bug fixes.

## Support

If you encounter any issues or have suggestions, please open an issue on GitHub or add "ncj" on Discord.

## Version History

### 1.9 Release
- Added "Start Heatseeker Freeplay" button to main menu and settings menu for instant heatseeker freeplay 
- Added "Enable Scoring" toggle using bCanBeScored memory flag (thanks to SoulDaMeep for the help!)
- Added one-click "Launch Heatseeker Practice" button (LAN match with no bots, unlimited time, 100 goal max)
- Plugin now LAN/Rocket Plugin MP only (removed exhibition mode support for stability and bug fixes - doesn't really make any difference)
- Fixed crash when toggling goal blocks during a match
- Goal blocker settings now persist between sessions

### 1.8 Release
Now works in multiplayer LAN using Rocket Plugin :)

### 1.7 Release
60fps bug now fixed :)

### 1.6 Release
Ability to now block both goals independently.
Includes bug fixes and optimizations from Acc70 (Thanks!).
shortDescription: A BakkesMod plugin that allows you to practice Heatseeker mode with blocked goals. Perfect for improving your Heatseeker skills in a free play environment.

The .csv file will be created at ./bakkesmod/data/StatisticallySpeaking-Matches-*SteamID*.csv, relative to the installation directory of the game and the Steam ID of the player.

### Changelog

#### 1.1
- Added MMR to match values
- Included Steam ID in filename

### Contact
 
Discord: Meggo#8033
shortDescription: Log statistics to a .csv file at the end of each match.

name: Rumble Tweaks
description:
## How To Use
You will need Rocket Plugin and Hamachi. This is very similar to any other modded match. All players must be on PC.
#### Host
Start a Rocket Plugin match. Make sure Rumble Tweaks is enabled. 
#### Guests
Join the Rocket Plugin match with the host???s Hamachi IP address. If you are lagging when using tweaked powerups, try installing and enabling Rumble Tweaks on your end.

## Changes
- Disruptor has been completely revamped, now gives your team full boost and other team no boost when used
- Disruptor is used up instantly
- Power Hitter now lasts for 6 seconds
- Power Hitter now gives you unlimited boost and 2x boost strength while active
- While you have Power Hitter, your supersonic speed is raised (i.e. you can go faster)
- Swapping now takes 0.25 seconds instead of 1 second

## Troubleshooting
#### Problem:
The tweaks aren't active!
#### Solution:
Make sure you have enabled Rumble Tweaks the in the BakkesMod plugins menu by pressing F2, clicking on Plugins, selecting Rumble Tweaks in the sidebar, and checking the "Enabled" box.

v0.3
shortDescription: A few tweaks to balance Rumble and reduce powerup cycling

name: Preset Shuffle
description:
Ever suffered from the (very real and not placebo) syndrome in which your car preset dictates how good you are at Rocket League?

If so, you might want to install Preset Shuffle! This plugin shuffles between your presets stored in your garage after a specific amount of games determined by the user, so you can automate your car changes and improve your gameplay (definitely data-proven)!

Features:
- Enable Plugin checkbox to toggle on/off the game-shuffle cycle.
- Lock Preset checkbox to keep your current preset and temporarily disable automatic game-shuffle cycle.
- Shuffle Preset button to instantly shuffle between your presets in your garage.
- Number of Games slider to determine how many games should pass between shuffling.
- Preset Selection window to choose specific presets to be enabled in the shuffle.
- (NEW) Cycle Preset added! Now you can choose to cycle sequentially through your presets rather than randomly shuffle.

Changelog:
(3/17/23) Bug fixes involving renaming and navigating renamed presets.
(5/15/23) Cycle Preset added, GUI/info changes.
(9/28/23) Check/Uncheck buttons added.
shortDescription: A plugin that shuffles between selected presets in your garage after a set amount of games.

name: Juggler Plugin
description:
# Juggler Plugin

### Description

I wanted to know how many times I could keep the ball in the air, as well as for how long, so I built a small tracker that displays those statistics. I found it also helpful and interesting for improving my dribbling.

### Features

* Keeps track of current, last, and best # of times you hit the ball before it touched the ground again.
* Keeps track of the current, last, best, and average amount of time the ball was in the air before it touched the ground again.
* Able to position the stats anywhere on your screen within the plugin menu.
* Able to reset the totals from within the plugin menu.

### Issues

* This plugin doesn't track 'all time highs' between play sessions, it's only the active play session.

If you find an issue or have a request, open an issue on GitHub please.
shortDescription: This plugin tracks the amount of times you hit the ball before it touched the ground, and how long it was in the air for

name: Custom Training Statistics (prev. Goal Percentage Counter)
description:
Keeps track of statistics during custom training in order to help you figure out if you are improving.

Get a quick overview of existing and new features:
https://youtu.be/d2tVlTZCGk8

Features:
- Display statistics about your current training session
- Display statistics per shot
- Count either goals or ability to hit the ball in time
- Customize the user interface
- Display goal speed
- Display a summary window of your training session
- Restore statistics after a break, crash, or the next day
- Display differences to your previous training session
- Advanced mechanics stats:
	- Ground Dribble Time
	- Air Dribble Time
	- Number of Air Dribble Touches
	- Number of Flip Resets
	- Average Flip Resets per attempt
	- Maximum Flip Resets in one attempt
	- Percentage of Flip Reset Goals vs Total Goals
	- Double Tap Goals
	- Percentage of Double Tap Goals vs Total Goals
	- Close Misses
	- Percentage of Close Misses
	- Restore previous stats for all advanced mechanics
	- Compare most advanced mechanics to previous session
- Toggle impact locations on the goal and backboard
- Toggle a heatmap of impact locations on goal and backboard
- Pause/Resume stat tracking, track in background
- Export Training Pack Info to clipboard
- Export Stat Summary to clipboard
shortDescription: Gathers and displays statistics about your custom training sessions

name: Quickchat Detoxify
description:
A Rocket League plugin that was made to block certain messages or quickchats. (This plugin does not work  on any other operating system than Windows, I'm not sure if bakkesmod is even supported on other OS)

(Comissioned by Crash)

###How to access the menu

You can access whenever by pressing the F2 button on your keyboard, heading over to Plugins on the top, then click on Quickchat Detoxify.

### How does it work

You can add text to be blocked (this blocks messages that contains the text, so if you block the word "hi", the word "high" gets blocked), you can also use the pre-existing list of in game quick chats, such as "What a save!" or "Wow!"

You can quickly enable or disable the feature to block the messages from a simple click of a button that can be accessed any time.

## Support

As of uploading this plugin, it hasn't been tested much so if you have any bug reports or glitches that you want fixed, shoot me a message on discord, you can also ask for certain features as well, my discord is: **oofsamy**
shortDescription: A Rocket League plugin that was made to block certain messages or quickchats to avoid people from tilting you and ruining your performance.

name: Replay Ball Cam Indicator
description:
This plugin will show ball cam indicator on replays. Supports custom indicator image. For people who want player view to be more like an in-game experience.

## **You might need to restart the game after installing the plugin for the first time.**

# How to Set Up Custom Indicator
1. Go to the BakkesMod folder by opening the injector, then file -> open BakkesMod folder
2. Go to data/ReplayBallCam folder
3. Replace the **"ballcam_indicator.png"** file for static indicator,
or replace **"ballcam_indicator_blinkbg.png"** and **"ballcam_indicator_blinkfg.png"** files for blinking indicator
4. You might need to restart the game after changing the indicator


# How to Toggle, Move, or Scale the Indicator
1. Open the in-game BakkesMod overlay using F2, go to plugins and select ReplayBallCam
2. Change the settings

Currently doesn't support ball cam toggle sound effect.

For alternate versions (higher resolution, with KBM toggle) of the indicator, go [here](https://imgur.com/a/jOqOF1r).

v1.1
- Animation support added (fade on toggle and blinks)
shortDescription: Shows ball cam indicator on replays.

name: Rocket League Minefield
description:
# As seen in [Rocket Sledge's video](https://youtu.be/Vyvc80eJkC4), this is Rocket League Minefield!

This plugin can only be used in Freeplay or in hosted matches through Rocket Plugin.

## Small boostpads
When the plugin is enabled, small pads will throw players' cars into the air in a range of force, height and rotation (adjustable in the plugin).

## Big boostpads
These are the deadly mines. A counter is attached to them, and every 6th time (based on the number of big boostpads in the field) any player picks up a big pad, the 6th "picker" will die. This can be turned off from the configuration.

### Showcase
You can check out this plugin at work in [Rocket Sledge's video](https://youtu.be/Vyvc80eJkC4) or in [my own video](https://youtu.be/daSyPqxx6uE)
shortDescription: Turn boostpads into deadly landmines!

name: AirDribble Indicator Ring
description:
### Plugin Details ###
This plugin renders a visual ring under the ball and markers on the ball surface to help you practice air dribbles and flip resets more consistently.

![Air Dribble Indicator Ring screenshot](https://i.postimg.cc/tC01tGf0/img1.png)


**Features:**

- Ring under the ball showing the ideal hit height to keep it in the air longer.
- Hit markers on the ball indicating where you actually made contact.
- Special color for detected flip reset touches.
- Configurable ring distance, thickness, and color.
- Configurable marker size, duration, maximum count, and colors (correct / incorrect / flip reset).

You can configure all settings from the plugin menu in BakkesMod (**F2 ??? Plugins ??? Air Dribble Indicator**).

![Air Dribble Indicator Ring config](https://i.postimg.cc/Gh0BJnf5/img2.png)


Usage example:
https://youtu.be/PnWI1qfslVs
shortDescription: Visual ring and hit markers to help practice air dribbles and flip resets.

tags.helpText: Improves/aids the usage of the game itself

name: MVPRPlugin
description:
MVPR is calculated as: Goals + (Assists * 0.75) + (Saves * 0.6) + (Shots / 3)

This is useful for those in 4Mans, or MLE, as this is a stat that is featured heavily in those communities.


**Version 1.1 update:**


Settings!!!


You can now change the opacity of the background, as well as its width, height, and position on screen!

Plugin can now easily be enabled/disabled

Option to have MVPR always showing, or keep it as toggle-able through the scoreboard button as usual.


ALSO!

MVPR will now show at the end of the match automatically, so you don't have to press the scoreboard button to make it show!


I hope you enjoy it!!!
shortDescription: This plugin shows the MVPR (MVP Rating) of everyone in the lobby when you look at the scoreboard, during or after the match.

name: Show Settings In Replay
description: Show the targeted players settings while viewing a replay.
shortDescription: Show Settings in Replay

name: Ball Possession
description:
Ball Possession is a plugin that allows you to display your team's Ball Possession during a game.
Ball Possession is calcalutated based on the position of the ball : 
- if the ball is in your half of the field, possession of the ball decreases.
- if the ball is in the opposing team's half, possession of the ball increases.
This way, this percentage shows how much you dominate the game, or contrary, how much you are in difficulty

In a second step, if you like the plugin and if you think it is relevant, I will add an option to change the method of calculation : the possession will be calcultated according to the time your team keeps the ball.
But I think it's less representative of the dynamics of the game.

Hope you will enjoy this plugin !

If you have any suggestion, don't hesitate to contact Myke38 on BakkesMod's Discord.
shortDescription: Show your team's Ball Possession during a match.

name: Spectator Replay Skip
description:
Do you find it annoying having to skip replays when you are a spectator in a private match?

Me too, But not anymore!

No configuration options. Just skip replays automatically when you are spectating.

All other replays will still work, so you can continue to force your opponents to watch your amazing bronze-level plays.

This plugin comes with:
* No bugs. Just skipping spectator replays.
* No more features, only skipping spectator replays.
* No guarantees of any kind other than skipping spectator replays.
* No hassle. Install it and start skipping spectator replays.

And you can have all of this absolutely free of charge!

Download your free copy today! Or don't... I just wanted to skip spectator replays myself.

name: Speed Drill Plugin
description:
A plugin that keeps track of the amount of time spent in a freeplay session and the amount of time between ball touches. Visibility/position/scale of the two timers is configurable in the menu, as are the thresholds for feedback between touches. By default, the ball timer stays green until 2 seconds, then yellow until 3, then red afterwards.

If you experience any issues about the plugin, or would like to request a feature/behavior change, please [file an issue](https://github.com/fabianvf/SpeedDrillPlugin/issues/new)
shortDescription: A plugin to track time between ball hits, to make running speed drills in freeplay easier.

name: Shooting By Wolf
description:
# Shooting by Wolf
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Hl3mrmdXK-s

This is a bakkesmod plugin designed similarly to Aim Training by Coco, where the target is a specific part of the net. 

The idea is to use this in a training pack or freeplay, where the shots can theoretically be varied and give more situations to train. 

## How to Install and Use
The name of the dll is NetToBallLine.dll, so that is the one you need to check in the plugin manager, and the name of the Plugin in the plugins tab is 'Shooting by Wolf'.
In the Plugin tab under the 'Shooting by Wolf', you can activate either the shooting game or the net to ball line. You can only use these in freeplay and in a training pack.

This was a lot of fun to make! Let me know if you have any feedback. Message me on Discord at wolf6160

## Sources
I wrote the game management, but I also used previously developed resources:
    -  Rendering Tools by CinderBlocc: https://github.com/CinderBlocc/RenderingTools/blob/master/
    - I used Martinii89's Bakkesmod Template: https://github.com/Martinii89/BakkesmodPluginTemplateV2
shortDescription: This is a shooting game designed for a training pack or freeplay.

shortDescription: Shot with your belly

name: Boost Roulette
description:
# Boost Roulette 

Russian roulette, but with boost. Everyone loves RNG in their competitive games.  

![Rocket League 2021 03 30 - 11 25 28 06 DVR](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8890971/113019258-65b90400-914f-11eb-9869-f81bb457c564.gif)

## [Check out my video of playing the mod with friends!](https://youtu.be/eNxjwGpDjk4)

---

## What is this?

When you pick up boost, there is a small chance it might backfire and blow you up!
Now you have to decide if getting that boost is worth the risk or continue alive without it. 

By default, the demo chance depends on the type of boost:  

- Big boost: 1 / 6 = ~16.7%
- Small pads: 1 / 28 = ~3.6%

You can easily change the probabilities in the plugin window.

## Is it safe to use?

Yes, but don't just take my word for it; the nice BakkesPlugins staff has verified it. The code is also open-source if you want to take a look for yourself :D 

## Have you downloaded this plugin before?

I rewrote the plugin in C++ (originally in Rust) for better QOL for the users and support.  
The auto-installer, plugin manager, etc., should all work now with the C++ installer.  
If you are not sure which version you have installed, uninstall it and install this one.

## How to Use

The Boost Roulette plugin is disabled by default. To enable it:

1. Open up the BakkesMod window (F2)
2. Go to the ```Plugins``` tab
3. Click on ```Boost Roulette Plugin```
4. Click the checkbox under ```Enable Boost Roulette Plugin```
  - This window is also where you can change the probabilities of demos on pickup for each boost type. 

Now, it should work!  
**If you are having any issues, go to the [full readme](https://github.com/Jakob-Strobl/boost-roulette).**

---

## Having trouble with the auto-installer?
Watch this video! https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EgsTUwlTjbM

--- 

## Have an idea for a plugin? 
If you have an idea for a plugin you want to see made, I am open for commissions! Add me on discord ```Strobl#2592```, and let's talk!
shortDescription: Russian roulette, but with boost.

name: Analog Directional Air Roll Visualizer
description:
`cl_airrollviz 1` to turn it on or `0` to turn it off.

The display turns green when analog directional air roll is used. It turns red when using the air roll toggle (default bindings). It is yellow whenever analog directional air roll is not being used, like when the input is 0 or 1, or when you're on the ground. You may find that it is yellow at value like 0.8 in the air. In that case, you are using an aerial sensitivity that causes the input to be effectively 1. (I am not just displaying the multiplied value because dodges will still use the unmultiplied value.)

This plugin was made so people using analog directional air roll can show it off and viewers can easily identify via the green color when it's being used. The red color for default air roll might also be useful to show off how different players use a combination of the two input methods.
shortDescription: This plugin can display the value of analog air roll and highlights in colors, whether directional or default airroll was used. Only works offline.

name: Goal Heatmap
description:
# GoalHeatmap
Rocket league plugin rendering heatmap of goals


I initially made this plugin to see if I had to train defense when the ball arrive on sides of the map.

By creating a heatmap of ball positions up to 10 seconds before a goal (you can choose), players can see if a particular position on the map is frequently less defended or too much favored when attacking.

Data is registered to a txt file each time a goal is scored and the heatmap regenerated at the end of each game (or manually via the plugin interface).


![Screenshot of the interface](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pierrejacquet/GoalHeatmap/master/screen.png)


Currently every goal scored is registered, so if you play with random teammates in 4v4, it will count too.
Anyway, statistically, it will be a good indication of your performance the more you play.
I'll probably update the plugin to add more filters (like game type, dates, etc) but I'm open to suggestions too.


The heatmap is generated using libheatmap - C library:  https://github.com/lucasb-eyer/libheatmap.

Feel free to mp me if you have any question or to report bugs (Peepan#1064 on Discord).

The (super dirty ugly) code is available on github [here](https://github.com/pierrejacquet/GoalHeatmap)
shortDescription: Render a Heatmap of all ball positions seconds before a goal has been scored (in your favor or not).

name: Training Pack Variance Manager
description:
Mode Name: Custom Training Pack Variance

UPDATE: Fixed several bugs when configs file would be emptied and some consistency issues for loading configs per pack.
Built in configs are updated up to 	Kevpert's Dodge Control 2 F3

Description:
The Custom Training Pack Variance mode provides a convenient way to quickly change the variance for each training pack in Rocket League. Instead of setting multiple key bindings per variance, this mode allows you to define custom variances for different training packs and easily switch between them.

Why it's useful:
- Quick Variance Adjustment: With Custom Training Pack Variance, you can swiftly modify the variance settings for each training pack. This flexibility enables you to fine-tune the difficulty, randomness, or specific parameters of individual packs without the need for complex configurations.

How to use:
1. Open Config File Directory: Click the "Open CFG Directory" button in the mode's settings panel. This will open the directory where the configuration file is located.

2. Edit the Config File: Open the configuration file using a text editor. Each line in the file represents a training pack and its associated variance settings. Follow the format:  ";;...". Add or modify the lines according to your desired variances for each training pack, and save the changes.

Note: The config file comes pre-configured with Kevpert training packs codes and matching variance settings, providing a starting point for customization.

3. Reload the Config File: After editing the configuration file, click the "Reload File" button in the mode's settings panel. This will load the updated variance settings from the config file.

4. Enable Custom Training Variance: Open the "Custom Training" tab in Rocket League and manually enable the "Custom Training Pack Variance" option. This will activate the mode and allow you to benefit from the custom variance settings.

Note: The Custom Training Pack Variance mode enhances your training experience and does not affect competitive play or official game modes. It provides a convenient way to adjust variances per training pack for personalized training sessions.

For any questions, suggestions, or support, feel free to reach out to me on TheGefen#8975. 

Enjoy your customized training sessions with ease using Custom Training Pack Variance!


Out of the box supported training packs:
GroundFlipReset 2 F11 555F-7503-BBB9-E1E3
Dodge Control 1 F7 2C44-12C9-F864-CF3B
Striking 1 F7 1711-49F1-66CB-FC65
Striking 2 F7 097C-9818-637B-7B55
Redirects F7 5E3A-FC4A-3AD7-0BBD
GroundFlipReset 1 F3AFE5-53FD-EC5B-9352
Adv Double Taps F10 3115-DC26-525D-F409
Adv Double Taps 2 F7 DEC7-4CAD-1452-30D3
Catches ground 1 F7 FC42-A3E1-A202-884A
PowerSlide F11 ADEB-B89D-7EC3-E7FA
Rebounds F12 9379-E2D6-673B-FFF3
Low Rebounds F11 950B-490B-649A-FBDE
Dodge Control 2 F3 0624-600D-000E-F2A7
shortDescription: The Custom Training Pack Variance mode provides a convenient way to quickly change the variance for each training pack in Rocket League

name: Watermark Plugin
description:
We have new image wrappers for plugins, so why not make something cool for people to use. Shoutouts to CinderBlock and Martinn for the feature and all of the core team for being awesome. Follow the instructions to add your logo on top of your game.

**INSTRUCTIONS**  
**BEFORE ENABLING MAKE SURE YOU'VE SET THE PLUGIN UP PROPERLY**  
Go to your bakkesmod folder - in your injector bakkesmod.exe hit file -> open BakkesMod folder  
It's found at %appdata%/bakkesmod/bakkesmod  
Open the data folder and add an image titled "watermark.png" with no quotes  
Then **RESTART YOUR GAME**  
Refreshing the plugin will cause a delayed crash of your game, so please just restart the whole game

Once you've reloaded the game and the plugin is loaded hit f2 -> plugins -> watermark plugin to set scale and x and y locations.

**Updated** to be compatible with a future injector change.  
**Updated** to add a background box to the plugin. Use it as a backing for your logo or as a scoreblock! Or just use this plugin as a scoreblock and skip the watermark. Commissioned by lemingtin

If you want to learn how to add images to your plugin, feel free to go to plugin homepage and steal my source code, It's super simple.

If you have a great idea and need an implementation DM or add me as friend on discord JerryTheBee#1117! I do plugin commissions!
shortDescription: Add a custom watermark to Rocket League

name: Rocket League Assistant
description:
# Rocket League Assistant
BakkesMod Plugin to integrate Rocket League events with Home Assistant

If you *really* want to buy me a coffee: [![ko-fi](https://ko-fi.com/img/githubbutton_sm.svg)](https://ko-fi.com/gtt1229)

## Discord: [Rocket League Assistant](https://discord.gg/yeJfg3t7fZ)

![RLABanner](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/49f5975c-f510-4585-8715-4d09a63ffd50)

## Special thanks to those in the Discord and notably [Branky](https://github.com/ItsBranK) and [JerryTheBee](https://github.com/ubelhj)
And some other person named Josh

# Home Assistant Configuration
The plugin utilizes Home Assistant's built in Webhook automation trigger with JSON-based conditions.

## Home Assistant Automations Configuration:

### [Option 1 - Generate the base automation using the in-game plugin settings window](https://github.com/Gtt1229/RocketLeagueAssistant#option-1---generate-the-base-automation-using-the-in-game-plugin-settings-window)
### [Option 2 - Create a new automation using the RocketLeague-BakkesBase.yaml file.](https://github.com/Gtt1229/RocketLeagueAssistant#option-2---create-a-new-automation-using-the-rocketleague-bakkesbaseyaml-file)
### [JSON Values](https://github.com/Gtt1229/RocketLeagueAssistant#json-values)

## Home Assistant Scenes Configuration to be Used in Automation(Not Required):

1. Create a new scene corresponding to the scenario (Home Team, Away Team, Demos, etc)
2. Give it a name (and icon/area if you'd like)
3. Add entities/devices to the scene and adjust the colors accordingly
4. Save the scene
5. Create a new, or duplicate the scene.
6. Add entities/devices to the scene and adjust the colors accordingly.
7. Save the scene
8. Repeat

[**More on scene creation here**](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/scene/)

### Notes

If you manually reload the plugin (through the F6 BakkesMod Console) while in the game, the URL will have to be reentered.

## To Do

* More JSON information
* Probably code clean up and Nullchecks
shortDescription: BakkesMod Plugin to integrate Rocket League events to Home Assistant or any smarthome/server than can receive and parse JSON data. The data can be used to change your game room's lights colors, or used for anything you can think of!

name: RLCSV Plugin
description:
RLCSV is a BakkesMod plugin that saves Rocket League scoreboard stats for all players to CSV files on the conclusion of an online match.

## Settings
* With Rocket League open press F2 -> Plugins -> RLCSVPlugin
* Save directory: The location where you would like your CSV files to be saved
  * Default is `bakkesmod/data/RLCSV/`
* Save directory structure: Saves CSV files into folders based on date and game mode. Deselecting this setting will save all CSV's regardless of date and game mode in the same folder
  * Example: `bakkesmod/data/RLCSV/2020-29-03/Standard/`

## Updates
**2020-04-02**:
* Add setting to change save directory
* Add setting to change save directory structure
* Add timestamp, game mode, and match result for each player in CSV file
shortDescription: RLCSV saves scoreboard stats into a CSV file at the end of a match

name: Ghost Plugin
description:
Ghost Plugin is a plugin that lets you record and race against ghosts of other players.

# Features :
- Record a ghost
- Choose the number of laps you want your run to be
- Export your ghost
- Import other ghosts from a .ghost file
- Replay an imported ghost
- Challenge an imported ghost (play the ghost while racing against it)
- Sort ghosts by number of laps
- Change the color of the ghost
- Auto-record when doing multiple tries
- Auto-export when your time is under a defined threshold

[A video of the features](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vU-1W7IzcBw)

For now, the only compatible map is [Ice Ice Outpost](https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2348278106)
shortDescription: A plugin to record and challenge Ghosts on racing workshop maps

name: Dropshot Calculated Shot
description:
# Dropshot Calculateshot
This is more or less a proof of concept. I am by no means qualified to say this is a viable strategy. This mod is only tested to play against bots. 

## General information:
- This mod is also a compilation of all the information from [DropShot Wiki](https://github.com/RLBot/RLBot/wiki/Dropshot)
- The highlighted hexagon that's always active is the balls prediction, AND the ball are colored. **RED** means the tile **WONT** be damaged, **GREEN** means the tile **WILL** be damaged. It may sound confusing, and your right. 
- This mod also predicts what tile the ball will hit.
- Ball down velocity needs to be >250 to be able to damage tiles.
- Car velocity towards ball needs to be >500 to do deal damage the ball.
- Accumulated Absorbed energy is fairly complicated, but its purpose is to stop the player from dealing to much damage to quickly. The ball ignores damage received in a 1 second time frame. More on this with better examples can be found on the [DropShot Wiki](https://github.com/RLBot/RLBot/wiki/Dropshot).
- You can only damage tiles after 0.2 seconds elapsed from the last time you damaged a tile.

If any dropshot pro's our seeing this mod and know of better strategy and care to share, slide into my dm's. 

[Demo video](https://youtu.be/Y-mbpgGNV6s)

Known issues:
- The visualization was set to my preference, so you'll have to deal with that.
- If the mod doesn't activate right away, press `f2` and go to plugin manager and look for `DropshotCalculateshot` and enable it.
- The hexagons don't clear right away after scoring, they do though.... eventually.
shortDescription: Calculates the best tile to shoot at in dropshot depending on the balls charge state.

name: RocketLink Plugin
description:
BakkesMod Plugin providing relative position data about other players in a Rocket League game for the [RocketLink Client](https://github.com/yannismate/RocketLink).  
The code for this plugin is heavily inspired by [SOS / Simple Overlay System](https://gitlab.com/bakkesplugins/sos/sos-plugin) made by some other great people. Check their repo out for more info.  

## WebSocket
This plugin runs a WebSocket server on port 49124 (configurable through the cvar "RLINK_Port") emitting relative player positions when in a local server.
```json
{
   "players":[
         {
            "distance": 1000,
            "id":"PLAYERID",
            "name": "PLAYERNAME",
            "team": 1,
            "__comment__": "This is the relative XYZ position of the other player to your own camera",
            "x": 0,
            "y": 0,
            "z": 0
         }
    ],
    "self_id":"YOUR OWN PLAYERID",
    "self_name":"YOUR OWN PLAYER NAME",
    "self_team": 1
}
```
shortDescription: BakkesMod Plugin providing relative position data about other players in a RocketLeague game for the RocketLink Client.

name: The Ultimate Saves Drill
description:
Follow our blog at https://bit.ly/lvl-up-blog for more Rocket League training tips, tools, and drills! We provide you with the tools to train smarter so that you can play better and rank up faster!

USAGE INSTRUCTIONS:
- Enter freeplay
- Press F6 to bring up the bakkesmod command line
- Type into the command line "beginSavesDrill" to start the saves drill. Your car will be placed in front of the net and the ball will start rapidly bouncing around in front of you. At a random point in time, the ball will be shot at you and you will need to react to the shot in order to save it. After either making contact with the ball or the ball going into the net, the next shot will start. The drill lasts for a total of 50 shots. 
- You can type "beginSavesDrill" again to reset the drill and run it again. You can also type "exitSavesDrill" at any time to exit out of the drill.

Use the difficulty slider in the plugin settings to adjust the average speeds of the shots, thus modifying the difficulty to fit your current skill level. Keep in mind that even small changes in the difficulty slider value can significantly alter the difficulty. 400 (the default value) is probably a decent number to try first, and then adjust up or down until you find the right fit. Over time, you should aim to:
a) Save a higher percentage of shots out of 50 at a given difficulty level; and/or
b) Save the same/similar percentage of shots at a higher difficulty level and continue increasing the difficulty level by 5-10 points every time you become consistent at a given difficulty.

***NEW FEATURE*** - Multiple car spawn points. - Enable this feature by checking the "Enable multi spawn locations" checkbox in the plugin window.
shortDescription: A rapid-fire drill to work on quickly reacting to unpredictable shots on goal

name: Player Switch
description:
# PlayerSwitch

PlayerSwitch is a Rocket League Plugin which switches the location of 2 players after x seconds.

## Installation

### Requirements

* The first thing you need is to install [Bakkesmod](https://www.bakkesmod.com/ "Bakkesmod")
* To play a custom game with others you need the [Rocket Plugin](https://bakkesplugins.com/plugins/view/26)

### Installing the Plugin

You can download the Plugin from the official Bakkesmod-Plugin Website [here](https://bakkesplugins.com/plugins/view/271)

# Configuration

## Configuration difference

| Type | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Static time | A switch with static time means every switch happens after the exact configured time in seconds |
| Dynamic time | A dynamic time switch signifies a switch between the 2 specified values. The switch happens after a random number between min and max |

## Configuration settings

### Default settings

| Setting | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| Enable | Enables the plugin |
| Enable for static | Enables the plugin for static times |
| Static time in seconds | The static time after which the switch happens |
| Enable for dynamic time | Enables the plugin for dynamic times |
| Dynamic time (min-max) in seconds | A minimum and maximum value between which the switch randomly happens |

### Other settings

| Setting | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| Enable velocity switching | Switches the speed of the players (recommended) |
| Enable car rotation switching | Switches the rotation of the players (recommended) |
| Enable boost switching | Switches the boost of the players |

### Example of my recommended settings (dynamic)
![configuration](https://media.discordapp.net/attachments/722569221283774484/891801202667421746/playerswitch_menu.png)

## Community
If you have any questions feel free to hop onto the [Bakkesmod Programming Discord Server](https://discord.com/invite/HMptXSzCvU) 

Alternatively you can contact me directly through discord using my tag: Oliver#1504
shortDescription: PlayerSwitch is a Rocket League Plugin which switches the location of 2 players after x seconds.

name: RocketLeagueRivals
description:
# Rocket League Rivals

Ever wonder if you've played against someone before or how often you run into a particular player? Curious about your win percentage against frequent opponents? That was my dilemma, and that's why I created Rocket League Rivals.

Rocket League Rivals is designed to track and display player interactions and statistics during Rocket League matches. This plugin provides detailed stats to help you analyze your performance against teammates and rivals, all calculated into a fun, though somewhat arbitrary, point system.

## Usage

The Rocket League Rivals plugin displays various stats about the players in your matches, presented in a customizable on-screen overlay.

### Displayed Stats

- **Player Name:** The name of the player.
- **Wins With:** Percentage of matches won with this player on your team.
- **Wins Against:** Percentage of matches won against this player.
- **Demos Given / Received:** Number of times you demolished this player and that this player has demolished you.
- **Rivalry Score:** A calculated score indicating the intensity of your rivalry with this player.

### Customization Options

- **Align Display Right:** Align the stats display to the right side of the screen.
- **Enable Logging:** Enable or disable logging of player stats to a file.
- **Hide My Team:** Hide stats for players on your team.
- **Hide Rival Team:** Hide stats for players on the opposing team.
- **Show All Wins/Losses Stats:** Show detailed win/loss stats for all players.
- **Show Team Stats:** Show team-based stats.
- **Show Demo Stats:** Show demolition stats.
- **Show Rival Stats:** Show rivalry scores for players.

The points are just for fun and serve as a way to keep track of your stats. You can also customize various settings within the plugin.

## TODO

Here are a few items on my list to enhance Rocket League Rivals:

- **Fix Logging to File:** The enable logging to a file option is currently not functioning as expected. While logs appear in the BakkesMod console, they do not write out to a file. I'm investigating this issue.
- **Database Integration:** I plan to add an option to track this data on a wider scale by integrating a database. Currently, the data is saved locally only.
shortDescription: Track stats against players you've played before.

name: SessionStats
description:
# **FEATURES**

Wins, losses, streak, and mmr change are tracked for the current session, with separate stats for each game mode.  These statistics can then be displayed either in-game, or written to text files for use in an OBS overlay as text sources (ask on Bakkesmod discord for more details if needed).

# **USAGE**

Access the plugin settings via Bakkesmod settings GUI (default F2->"Plugins" Tab->SessionStats)

"Reset" button will reset stats across all modes to zero.

Default destination for OBS text source output is: "bakkesmod/data" (note forward slashes are always used when designating a path), which saves the files to:
SteamsteamappscommonrocketleagueBinariesWin32bakkesmoddata


# **ISSUES**

- as noted above, forward slashes (e.g. the '/' character) must be used for paths. Backslashes can cause trouble with config file saving.
- changes to a new mode are not detected until opening kickoff (can cause confusion when joining a casual game in progress)
- first placement match in any mode registers as a win, since MMR delta is always positive
shortDescription: Open Source plugin: Tracks and displays wins/losses/streak/mmr for a Rocket League session.  Options for both in-game screen display, and OBS integration, as well as keeping separate statistics for each game mode.

name: BakkesMod Graphs
description:
`This plugin is maintained by the BakkesMod team.`

This plugin takes away the headache of manually building graphs within BakkesMod. With this plugin and its source code, it is now very easy to display live data on screen. There are examples provided in the GraphingPluginExamples project in the BMGraphs github repository. You can get to this repository by clicking the View Plugin Homepage button.

Included with the download is the source code for the graphs. You can find these files in `/bakkesmod/data/BMGraphs/`.  You will need to include these 3 files in a plugin:
- BMGraphs.h
- BMGraphs.cpp
- BMGraphsMacrosStructsEnums.h

The `BMGraphs` class should be stored as a `shared_ptr` member variable in your plugin class, and should be constructed in the onLoad function.

If you have any questions, feel free to ask in the programming channel within the BakkesMod Discord server.
shortDescription: Quick data visualization for plugin developers

name: KeepTraining
description:
Never waste time between matches again! This BakkesMod plugin automatically launches Freeplay, Custom Training or Workshop maps sessions at the end of Rocket League matches, helping you maintain practice momentum and improve more efficiently.

## Join our discord
Please consider joining our [discord](https://discord.gg/ycrbhbAKaK) if you have any questions, feedbacks or suggestions, I'd be happy to help!

## **Features**
- Automatic transition to training sessions after matches
- Support for Freeplay, Custom Training, and Workshop maps
- Customizable training pack selection
- Customizable Workshop map selection
- Wait for MMR calculation before transitioning
- Optional requeue delay for optimal training time
- Auto requeue functionality to get back into matches
- Mode-specific settings (Casual, Ranked)
- Multilingual support (English and French)

## **Options**
**Language** - Define the plugin language (English or French supported)  
**Enable/Disable Plugin** - Toggle the plugin's functionality on or off  
**Training Mode** - Choose between Freeplay, Custom Training or Workshop maps (requires WorkshopMapLoader plugin)  
**Custom Training Pack Code** - Specify which training pack to load (for Custom Training mode)  
**Workshop map** - Specify which workshop map to load at the end of a match  
**Auto Requeue** - Automatically requeue for matchmaking after training  
**Requeue Delay** - Set how long to train before requeuing (in seconds)  
**Wait for MMR** - Wait for MMR calculation to complete before transitioning to training  

### Game Mode Settings
Enable or disable the plugin for specific game modes:
- Casual Matches
- Ranked Matches

## **Commands**
Access the plugin with the following console commands:
- `kt_toggle` - Toggle enable/disable the plugin
- `kt_enabled [0/1]` - Enable/disable the plugin
- `kt_requeue [0/1]` - Toggle auto requeue
- `kt_requeue_delay [seconds]` - Set requeue delay
- `kt_wait_mmr [0/1]` - Toggle waiting for MMR calculation before transitioning to training

## **Compatibility**
The plugin transitions you to training after:
- Casual matches
- Ranked matches

**Note**: For Workshop maps functionality, the WorkshopMapLoader plugin must be installed.

## **Planned Features**
- Randomize training pack/workshop map from a customizable list
- Support for additional game modes
- More language options

## **Troubleshooting**
If the plugin isn't working as expected:
- Check that the plugin is enabled in the BakkesMod settings
- Verify that the correct training pack code is entered (for Custom Training)
- Make sure the game mode you're playing is enabled in the plugin settings
- For Workshop maps, verify that the WorkshopMapLoader plugin is installed
- Restart BakkesMod and Rocket League if settings aren't applying correctly

## **Feedback and Support**

For bugs, feature requests, or general help:
- Discord: [Join our server](https://discord.gg/ycrbhbAKaK)
- Donate: [PayPal](https://paypal.me/joanbgadev)
shortDescription: Never waste time between matches again! This BakkesMod plugin automatically launches Freeplay, Custom Training or Workshop maps sessions at the end of Rocket League matches, helping you maintain practice momentum and improve more efficiently.

name: Wall Dash Trainer
description:
# Wall Dash Trainer

A simple timer that shows how close you are to successfully performing wall dashes.

To perform wall dashes, you need to be able to double jump with roughly 90 ms or less between the jumps.
Works in freeplay and custom training.

This plugin was modified from the [Fast Aerial Trainer [Improved]](https://bakkesplugins.com/plugins/view/514) that was originally made by Vync and improved by Josef37.

![walldashtrainer](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d870d839-0c42-4ea4-adce-e1cc5c09e0df)



## Features

- Show time spent holding initial jump
- Show time between starting initial and double jump
- Timers are affected by freeplay game speed
- Customizable ranges for green, yellow and red portions of the timers
- Customizable GUI colors
- Customizable GUI position and scale

## Wall dashing

There are different methods for wall dashing. I have only tested this plugin with the following method:

1. Drive on a wall.
2. Hold the left analog stick forward.
3. Hold air roll right or left depending on which wall you are on. 
The point is to perform a diagonal flip towards the wall.
4. Tap jump twice as fast as you can.
5. Check [Speedometer](https://bakkesplugins.com/plugins/view/73) to verify that you are gaining speed.

## Practicing with slow motion

To make wall dashing easy, reduce freeplay speed to 50% - 90%. 

The game speed also affects the times shown by this plugin.
The timers show game time, not real time.

## Explanation of Values

### Hold First Jump

Duration jump button was held down on your initial jump.  
Faster is presumably better.

Holding the first jump down too long will not leave you any time to perform the second jump.

### Time Between Jumps

Time from first jump button press to second jump button press.  
This value includes the time spent holding down the initial jump.  
Faster is better.

Target is 80 ms or less. But values up to 95 ms has worked during testing.




## Changelog

See [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/debnera/WallDashTrainer/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md).
shortDescription: Wall dashes require very fast double jumps. This plugin tells you how fast you are clicking the jump button.

name: KPH Challenge
description:
The **KPH Challenge** is a Rocket League plugin for **BakkesMod** that enhances your freeplay experience by tracking and visually displaying the goal speeds you've achieved. Each goal scored unlocks a tile corresponding to its speed in KPH, and scoring the same speed repeatedly will progressively darken the tile???showing your progress toward mastery.

The plugin persistently saves your achievements, including goal speeds, repeat counts, and total attempts, ensuring you maintain progress even after restarting Rocket League.

> ** Important:**  
> For this plugin to function correctly, make sure **"Disable Goal Reset"** in settings is turned **OFF**.

### Available Commands:
- `/kphchallenge_fullreset`  
  **Description:** Fully resets your unlocked speeds, repetition counts, and attempt totals. Ideal for starting fresh or challenging yourself anew.

### Key Features:
- Visually engaging speed-tracking grid
- Persistent storage across game sessions
- Gradually darkening tiles to indicate repeated successes
- Attempt counter displayed clearly on-screen
shortDescription: Visually track and challenge your Rocket League goal speeds in freeplay.

name: Versatile Training
description:
Versatile Training
Advanced Training Tools for Rocket League

Versatile Training is a comprehensive BakkesMod plugin designed to enhance your Rocket League training experience with powerful customization features.

Core Features
Custom Training Packs: Create, modify, and share advanced training scenarios with precise control over car and ball positioning, boost amounts, and more

Snapshot System: Save and load game states from replays or training to recreate specific moments for practice


Goal Blockers: Set up custom-sized goal blockers for precision shooting practice and target training

Pack Overrides: Customize existing training packs, even those by other creators

Versatile Training Hub: Community platform for sharing and discovering training packs


Advanced Features


Recording & Playback (Experimental): Record your inputs when taking shots and spawn bots that replay your exact movements

Precise Position Control: Fine-tune car position, rotation, and velocities for creating perfect training scenarios

Variance Settings: Add randomization to shots for more realistic practice

Replay Integration: Create training packs directly from replay snapshots


Getting Started

Open the plugin interface with F3 (default)

Create or load a training pack from the "Custom Training Packs" tab

Use the "Snapshot Gallery" to save and load specific game states

Configure goal blockers and other settings in the Settings tab


Community Features

Visit the [Versatile Training Hub](https://versatile-training-hub.vercel.app/) to discover and share training packs with the Rocket League community.


As a note, there are still bugs in this plugin and a lot of weird edge cases, this is the biggest project I've ever done, and I've just been putting off releasing until it's absolutely perfect. Had a change of heart so I thought I might as well let people use it already, because it is in a pretty good place.

Here is a demo video of everything in the plugin

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9eDVzMzTQ7c

(note, credit to [dark penguin](https://www.reddit.com/r/RocketLeague/comments/65w06v/things_i_would_love_to_see_in_custom_training/) for the banner, ripped it from his reddit post like 8 years ago)
shortDescription: Unlock Custom training options to enhance your training

name: Jake's Flip Directional Indicator
description:
Tired of mis-flipping or whiffing by flipping incorrectly? With this plugin, you get simple instant feedback on how you flipped. Once your car flips it will tell you the exact angle you flipped towards. It even comes with a GUI dial to point in the direction of your flip. It's great for lower ranks to lock in fundamentals, and even higher ranks to practice/learn new mechanics such as dashes.

## Update Log:
- 2/8/25: Fixed bug where dial wasn't loading 
- 2/11/25: Now works in-game and added position sliders and removed extraneous code
- 2/12/25: 
   + Removed in-game functionality (Utilized hook event caller is not allowed in online games and caused game crash + 5 min ban)
   + Fixed bug where the background square is in strange locations on the screen
- 3/26/25:
   + Added color customization
   + Includes saved presets you can customize
shortDescription: Simple plugin for feedback on flip direction

name: NextGame
description:
Antisnipe for streamers :

- It randomly triggers the queue in a preselected timeframe, and displays an upper banner to hide the queuing bar.
- NEW : Sounds that hints about queuing are disabled (Start match search + 3...2...1... countdown) -- credits to SoulDaMeep

**The plugin is disabled by default. 
To enable it, open bakkesmod settings (F2), Plugins tab, NextGame settings, and check "Enable plugin".** 

# Settings

- **Enable plugin** : Enables the plugin.
- **NEW : Auto queue after a game ends (Only applies to ranked games)** : Queues automatically 2 seconds after a game ends, only in ranked (to avoid queing after casual, private or tournament games)
- **Enable Instant Freeplay when queueing** : Teleports in freeplay as soon as the key to start the search is pressed or when Auto Queue is enabled.
- **Select Playlist** : The playlist you want to play
- **Key to start/stop delayed queuing** : The key to start/stop the random game search *(Try to avoid a letter or a common key, because if you have the instant freeplay on and you press the key because you write in the chat at the end of the game, you will teleport in freeplay :D. I personnaly use the Numpad 0 (Keyboard-Numpad zero)*)
- **Min and Max delay defore queuing (in seconds)** : Minimum and maximum time to wait before triggering the queue. *(1,60) means a random queue within the minute*
- **Simulate hiding bar (for customization)** : Simulate the upper banner to hide the queuing bar. Helps to customize the settings below. Don't forget to uncheck this setting when you are done customizing.
- **Hiding bar text** : Text to display in the upper banner (accented and specials characters wont work)
- **Hiding bar text color** : Color of the text to display
- **Hiding bar text size** : Size of the text to display
- **Hiding bar height** : Height of the upper banner. Needs to be manually adjusted depending your screen resolution, UI percentage, etc ...

name: Time Tracker Plugin
description:
## Description
This plugin is made to track how much time you spend in each map. This is cool information to have especially for seeing how much time you've spent in different custom maps. You can sort the table by map name or time played and reverse each of the sorting methods.

## How It Works
The timer starts when you join a map and ends either when you leave the map or the podium at the end of the game is shown. Maps are identified by their package names, which aren't always the most accurate for what we commonly call them. I've provided map names for the included maps in the game such as Beckwith Park and whatnot. You can overwrite all the names for maps by double clicking on it in the timer table. This will display a popup where you can edit the name to whatever you like.
shortDescription: A plugin that tracks how much time you've spent in each map

name: Teleport Stuff
description:
The whole point of this was to have a GUI over the console calls to 'player' or 'ball'.
If you go to the plugin settings tab, I have the same thing rendering there as the window would. The plugin itself is called `tpmanager`. 

You can click+drag the individual fields but it's finicky. You can lower the polling time or I'd just recommend to double clicking or shift click and type a value in.

There are a couple teleport features if you're just exploring a giant map and wanna congregate some players:
 - The first combo box designates what will be teleported
 - The second combo box says where things will be teleported
 - The "to" button means it'll place all objects at the destination's coordinates
 - The "above" button means it'll place objects in a vertical column to avoid collisions at the coordinates.

If you prefer the window you can bind `togglemenu tpmanager`

Github: https://github.com/Naoki95957/RocketLeagueTPManager/tree/master

name: Hot Potato
description:
# Hot Potato

Bakkesmod plugin to help practice bounce dribbling.
*This plugin only works in freeplay!*

## What is Hot Potato

Hot potato is a practice drill inspired by [SpookLuke](https://youtu.be/1ggYEBQyiRk?t=250), where the goal is to let the ball bounce but not let it bounce more then twice.

This plugin adds a overlay that shows how often the ball has bounced since the last time, your current streak and the high score of the current session.

While the purpose of a practice drill isn't to reach a high score, it can be nice to see how well you are doing and be able to have a metrics to compare to over a period of time.

## Using the plugin

To use the plugin head over to the plugins section, then head over to the Hot Potato section.

Then press the `Toggle Hot Potato overlay` button, which will show a small overlay showing how often the ball has bounced, your current streak and highscore of the active session.

You can also add a custom keybind to open this menu `togglemenu HotPotato`

## Support & Bugs
Something not working for you? Or have you discovered a bug? Or do you have a suggestion that would make this plugin even better?
Reach out to me on Discord:
AnxySlender#1337

## Changelog
### V1.0.0
- Add setting for max bounces allowed
- Add button reset the session highscore
shortDescription: aims to help you practice bounce dribbling

name: Hell In A Cell Scoreboard
description:
# What is Hell In A Cell?
Hell in a Cell is a fun, demo-oriented gamemode designed by the [Boom Squad](https://discord.gg/zqkzBJEXFF), hosts of the [Demolition Leaderboard](https://ubelhj.shinyapps.io/demobotR/). It can be played in normal Private Matches, but has its own scoring and rules. Instead of goals being the only way to get points, demolitions do too! Demolitions are worth 1 point, Exterminations are 3 points, and Goals are 5 points.

The plugin tracks the stats of each team, and shows the Hell in a Cell scores on screen while you're playing.

Join the [Boom Squad Discord](https://discord.gg/zqkzBJEXFF) if you're interested. We host tournaments twice a year.

Stream friendly! Outputs files for OBS in .txt format as you play in a folder `%appdata%/bakkesmod/bakkesmod/data/HellInACell/`.

## Updates
- 5/1/21 
	- Posted as verified plugin.
	- Works with non-English Rocket League 
	- Generates its own .set file.
	- Adds custom colored backgrounds to in-game stat displays
	- Adjustable font size for in-game stat overlay
	- Plugin works for spectators automatically
	- Change the names of stats in the in-game overlay
		- ex: `hic_set_render_exterminations "Exterms: "` will shorten Exterminations to Exterms
	- Can disable in-game overlay while still outputting stats for OBS
shortDescription: Tracks your score for the Hell In A Cell Gamemode

name: RLStatSaver
description:
# RL Stat Saver

RLStatSaver is a simple stats saver for Rocket League.
It will track team color, username, goals, assists, saves, shots, demolitions, score, MMR, team score, Wins and Losses, timestamps, platform, and unique ID for each player.

## Set Up

- There is very little setup for this plugin. Simply install it and it should automatically track and save your match data.
- If you need help installing the plugin, go to [bakkesplugins.com](https://bakkesplugins.com/).

## How to Access Data

- At the end of each match, RLStatSaver will save each game's data to a .csv file in the bakkesmod data directory.
- The files are grouped by playlist and year.
- To find this directory, go to `%appdata%/bakkesmod/bakkesmod/data/RLStatSaver/{year}` and each `.csv` file will be named according to playlist.

## Use Cases & Other Info

The idea is this app will be acting as a local data repository for your Rocket League games.
It provides the flexibility to analyze trends, review past games for fun, and otherwise manipulate the file to your liking. No external services required.

If you found any bugs or something just let me know on Discord: @jdhrl
shortDescription: RLStatSaver is a Rocket League plugin that tracks and saves match stats locally.

You can also set a timer after which the plugin will kick you out of Rocket League. Don't worry, it will let you finish any online mode (i.e. ranked) before quitting.

UPDATE - 
4 modes are available now

2) Quit after set amount of losses 
3) Quit after set amount of net losses (i.e. losses - wins)

You can now automatically bind the chosen mode to activate/deactivate.

name: QuickSuite
description:
# QuickSuite
QuickSuite is a BakkesMod plugin that allows users to automatically requeue or join a map of their choice.  
This plugin was heavily inspired by existing plugins (Instant Suite and Auto Leave)

# Features
This plugin lets you instantly join:
- Freeplay 
- Training 
- Workshop  


### Freeplay_Map
By using the drop down, you can choose the map you want to join after a match/game from the updated map list.  

Just choose a map and make sure the plugin is activated!

### Training_Map
Unlike existing plugins, QuickSuite allows you to join a training map using a training map code.  
To join a training map, enter the map code and a name for the map. This adds it to your training map list, which you can access through the combo box.  
Simply select the map of your choice and you can load into it after a match.

### Workshop_Map
QuickSuite lets you automatically join workshop maps after a match, provided they are already installed on your system.  
The plugin automates the **joining** process, while downloading and managing the maps is handled separately (see [Prerequisite](#prerequisite)).

# Prerequisite
You first need [BakkesMod](https://www.bakkesmod.com/) for this to work  
To use the **Workshop_Map** feature, you must install the [Workshop Map Loader & Downloader](https://bakkesplugins.com/plugins/view/223) plugin.  

- Use the Workshop Map Loader to **download** maps.  
- Ensure the maps are stored in the correct directory:  
C:\Program Files\Epic Games\rocketleague\TAGame\CookedPCConsole\mods  
- Once maps are downloaded and in the proper location, QuickSuite can load them automatically after matches.

# Setup
1. First open BakkesMod using the F2 key, then locate the plugins tab  
![](images/rl1.png)  

2. Then locate the QuickSuite plugin and select it  
![](images/rl2.png) 

3. Enable the plugin by clicking the checkbox, you can also choose to requeue by clicking the appropriate checkbox  
![](images/rl3.png) 

4. If you want to jump into a freeplay map, click the appropriate radio button and use the combo box to select the desired map  
![](images/rl4.png) 

5. If you wnat to jump into a training map, click the appropriate radio button and add a training map to your list.
Use the textboxes to add the map's code and name. Click the Add Training Map button to add.
![](images/rl5.png)  
You can now access that map in the combo box, allowing you to load in after a match  
![](images/rl6.png)  

6. If you want to jump into a workshop map, click the appropriate radio button and the combo box should populate with the maps that you have already downloaded
![](images/rl7.png)

## Disclaimer
This is my first plugin, so if you have any advice or recomendations feel free to contact me or clone by repo and code your own changes!  
Thanks! :)
shortDescription: Instantly join freeplay, training or workshop maps after a match!

name: Force Training
description:
This plugin allows you to force a Goalie or Striker custom training pack to be a specific mode (Goalie or Striker). Normally, Goalie training packs stop attempts after the ball is cleared. For training purposes, players may want to continue clearing the ball and transition from defense to offense to practice their control. This pack allows you to play Goalie training packs in Striker mode. When enabled, the plugin's settings are applied every time a training pack is loaded.

## How to use

### Plugin Settings Interface
The plugin settings can be managed through the `Plugins` tab. This menu can be accessed using the default `F2` menu. Navigate to `Plugins > Force Training` to access the settings page for the plugin.

### Console Variables / CVars
The plugin introduces two console variables you can control through the default `F6` console. The variables are `ft_enabled` and `force_training_type`. These plugins control whether the plugin is enabled and which training mode is enforced, respectfully.

* ft_enabled - This variable can take a value of either 0 or 1. Example: `ft_enabled 1` to enable the plugin.
* force_training_type - This variable can take a value of either 1 (Striker) or 2 (Goalie). Example: `force_training_type 1` to force Striker training for custom training packs.

## Miscellaneous
After installation, you may need to run `plugin load forcetraining` in the default `F6` console. 

## Bugs/Issues
For issues/bugs contact [@WatchProsPlay](https://twitter.com/WatchProsPlay)

It is recommended to disable the plugin when not in use to prevent conflicts with other custom training pack plugins. Please report any issues through the details below.
shortDescription: Forces Striker/Goalie Mode For Custom Training Packs

name: Best Game Mode Splitted MMR!
description:
You play many competitive different games like 1vs1, 2vs2, 3vs3   or you just select multiple modes and find the first available, So, this plugin is for you.

It will track the amount of MMR you won/lost for each of them. indicating your actual rank  and how much MMR is left to promote to the next division or rank

It works on Multiplayer Competitive Games

It display a Stats Table swith your MMR in 1vs1, 2vs2 & 3vs3 Competitive playlists.

 It contains your wins, losses, gained MMR (or lost if negative), your current Rank and how much MMR is left to promote to the next division (or Rank if you are in Division IV )

The stats table will be visible at the main menu and with the Scoreboard in the middle of your games.

The stats table's position (x, y) is configurable.
It also has a checkbox to enable or disable the plugin without needing to go to Plugin Manager.

***NOTE 1:*** Name of the plugin:   jdeMMRTracker.

***NOTE 2:*** Be aware that leaving a match before it officially ended, could result in inaccurate stats.

***NOTE 3:*** Your actual rank is 1 or 2 letter/s then  a "D" and then the Division number
e.g. "G3 D2"   Means:   "Gold 3 Division II"

Ranks:  UNR,  B,  S,  G,  P,  D,  CH,  GC,    stand for   Unranked, Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond, Champion, Grand Champion,  
and if you are a SSL ...  well, I don't see any reason for this plugin...  There is nothing to promote to the next level... 

Have Fun & Enjoy it!!
shortDescription: MMR Tracker for 1vs1 2vs2 and 3vs3 individually. It also tells you Actual Rank per game mode and MMR To promotion.

name: Curveball plugin
description:
ATTENTION: you might have to exit and re-enter freeplay one time for the effect to be seen. will try to fix that soon

applies new physics forces to the ball to add curving trajectories in the air based on spin


## Commands
**sv_soccar_curve 0|1**   -- turn the effect on and off

**sv_soccar_drag_coefficient [float]**  -- set the drag coefficient , this is 1 of two parameters that effect the ball flight

**sv_soccar_lift_coefficient [float]** -- affects how much the curve turns the ball

**sv_soccar_lift_zmod [float]** -- multiplier for the vertical curving

**sv_soccar_maxspin [float]** -- multiplier for the maximum spin speed the ball can have

**sv_soccar_lift_ground 0|1** -- turns horizontal curving on when the ball is on the ground

**sv_soccar_draw_magnus_force 0|1** -- draw some little red lines showing which way the magnus force is pushing the ball

**sv_soccar_magnus_debug 0|1** -- when activated, autospins the ball very fast, to ensure you see a visible effect on the ball

**sv_soccar_forcemode - 0|6** -- specify the method of adding force to the ball
    
    Force               = 0,
    Impulse             = 1,
    Velocity            = 2,
    SmoothImpulse       = 3,
    SmoothVelocity      = 4,
    Acceleration        = 5,
    MAX                 = 6
shortDescription: Adds Magnus force to the ball, as seen in SunlessKhan's video

name: Post Percentage
description:
# Post Percentage

Post Percentage is a BakkesMod plugin that counts the amount of posts you hit in a given session (from booting the game to closing it). It tracks in Freeplay, Custom Training, and Online games but only for the Soccar gamemode. It also gives you the ability to mute or change the crossbar ping audio (currently only muting supported)
It will only track the shot if the player running the plugin had the last touch on the ball, and it only counts the initial shot from the player (ie, not tracked if the shot was off-target, but own-goaled by a defender).
The stats can be reset using the console command 'ResetShotCounterStats', or the button in the plugin panel.
The "post size" can be modified through the slider in the plugin panel, the default is 100uu
Expanded stats (goals/game, posts/game, own goal stats, etc) are available in the BakkesMod plugin panel. Please contact 'mdottwo2' on Discord for any bugs, questions or requests.

To be added later:
-> custom crossbar sound options
-> Long term storage of shot history per session
-> Shot tendency recommendations
shortDescription: See just how bad at shooting you are

name: Rocket League BotChat
description:
![Rocket League BotChat](https://briancaffey.github.io/img/rlbc/cover.png)

Rocket League BotChat gives Rocket League bots that ability to chat based on in-game events.

It is designed to be used with a large language model running locally on your PC. You can set this up using NVIDIA's TensorRT-LLM library. The plugin requires an OpenAI-compatible API server running on localhost:5001. Please see this repository for details on how to set up a TensorRT-LLM-powered large language model on Windows: https://github.com/NVIDIA/trt-llm-as-openai-windows.

This project was developed for NVIDIA's Gen AI on RTX PCs Developer Contest.

For more information about this plugin, please see this article on my blog: https://briancaffey.github.io/2024/02/17/rocket-league-botchat-nvidia-generative-ai-on-rtx-pcs-developer-contest.
shortDescription: Rocket League BotChat is a BakkesMod plugin for Rocket League that allows bots to send chat messages based on in-game events in offline matches.

name: RL Win Ratio Tracker
description:
RL Win Ratio Tracker saves a user's match data (e.g., goals scored, saves made) to calculate win percentage. This plugin makes it simple to check how a user's odds of winning are affected by things like how many goals they scored in a match.

UPDATE 1.0.1 (03/24/24) - Resolved a bug that caused the game to crash when changing the sliders.

UPDATE 1.0.2 (09/25/25) - Resolved a couple more bugs that caused game crashes.
shortDescription: This plugin tracks a user's win percentage based on certain match statistics.

name: TrainingPerformanceGraphs
description:
# TrainingPerformanceGraphs [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)

**TrainingPerformanceGraphs** is a BakkesMod plugin that automatically tracks the user's performance in all rocket league training packs. The plugin also lets the user graphically see improvements or deteriorations to their performance in **shooting score percentages, boost usage, and goal speed**. 

## This plugin can help you:
- **Visually see your improvements:** Automatically and simply measure your key performance metrics.
- **Assist you in training:** Focus on one aspect of your performance at a time.
- **Precise metrics:** Know exactly when you've reached your desired performance level.

## Installation
1. Install [BakkesMod](https://www.bakkesmod.com/download.php).
1. Ensure BakkesMod is open in background.
1. Install [TrainingPerformanceGraphs](https://bakkesplugins.com/plugins/view/505) plugin.
1. Launch Rocket League.

**You are now automatically tracking your training session metrics!**

## View and Configure Graphs
Press 'F2', goto 'Plugins' goto 'TrainingPerformanceGraphs'

From the drop down, select desired training pack (if you have no data yet there are 5 randomly generated example data sets you can use).

To toggle graphed line(s) for all available shot(s), click "Add all shots" or "Remove all shots" respectively.

![toggleall](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/618516a9-6776-4dad-906b-4e521ae7c5e4)

To add graphed line for a specific shot(s), enter the shot number in the text box, then click the "Add Shot" button.

![addspecificshot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/37267ed1-6ebf-4a48-b2f3-1bd151848989)

You can hover over shot legend labels to highlight a corresponding graphed line. You can also click on the legend label icon color in order to toggle their visibility.

![legendlabels](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c80d1ed3-6237-4b39-a62b-7dcc26f6713b)

### Manually read your training data:
*Training Session (User XML and Generated Example XML) Data is written to:*

    C:UsersUSERNAMEAppDataRoamingbakkesmodbakkesmoddata
shortDescription: Automatically track your custom training data, visualize your improvements!

name: Tachometer Display
description:
# Tachometer Plugin for BakkesMod

A clean, minimal tachometer HUD plugin for Rocket League using BakkesMod. Displays your current car speed as a radial gauge during freeplay, replays, and post-goal sequences.

## Features

-  Real-time tachometer (speedometer) gauge in KM/H
-  Supersonic speed threshold highlighting
-  Compatible with Freeplay, Replays, Goal Replays
-  Configurable activation via CVar `plugin_enabled`
shortDescription: Displays car speed in KM/H in a clean tachometer style HUD

name: Joystick Viewer
description:
This plugin takes the basic functionality of the [Joystick Self-Check](https://bakkesplugins.com/plugins/view/54) plugin by @AlpacaFlightSim and adds a ton of customization options and some new features.

# Input History after Jump:  
When practicing/performing complex flip cancel moves it can be useful to see why something worked or didn't work.  

This plugin can store a variable number of joystick input values before and after each jump input and then display them, so you can see why a flip cancel or other move did or didn't work.

# Customizability:
This plugin features extensive customizability:
- Adjust size and location of view
- Adjust size of icons indicating each joystick position coordinate
- Adjust size of lines connecting the icons
- Adjust color of the outer box
- Adjust color of first and last input for each view (normal, pre-jump input, and post-jump input). Intermediate inputs have their color mapped linearly forming a nice gradient.
   - Note you can also adjust the alpha channel in the color picker for more options
- Adjust length of the input trail
- Adjust how long the input history display is frozen after a jump input
- Customize what modes it shows up in (freeplay/custom training, local match, online match)

# Similar Plugins
- [Joystick Visualization](https://bakkesplugins.com/plugins/view/516) by @Brotzeitsepp
   - Also adds features and customizability to the original [JoystickSelfCheck](https://bakkesplugins.com/plugins/view/54) by @AlpacaFlightSim
   - I created my plugin before realizing this one existed. 
   - @Brotzeitsepp's features additional options for showing aerial sensitivity adjustments and deadzone highlighting, however it does not feature a freeze-frame when a jump occurs and lacks some customizability like line size and gradient display options
shortDescription: Customizable joystick input viewer for verifying joystick quality and seeing the joystick position history immediately surrounding a jump input.

name: DARMetronome
description:
A plugin that starts a metronome when using Directional Air-Roll.

The plugin will start a metronome when pressing Directional Air-Roll in freeplay and keep it going while it is hold down. It will play a sound on each beat and display a bar showing the progress of the revolution.

The idea from this plugin and its purpose is to help training with the [Losfeld Method of Directional Air-Roll](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NTOBUcqFLVs).

**Note: It is fixed on the neutral revolution with a fixed camera, not to the car's revolution. If the camera moves, it will no longer work as intended.** It can help for the exercises in free play, but is not adequate for rings maps.
shortDescription: A plugin that starts a metronome when using Directional Air-Roll, to help training with the Losfeld Method of Directional Air-Roll in freeplay.

name: Python Bindings
description:
This is a plugin that enables the development and use of python in bakkesmod plugins.

Any python script placed in `bakkesmoddatapython` will be attempted to load. The plugin will call `onLoad(GameWrapper, CVarManagerWrapper)` when loading the script. The plugin constructor should be called here.

Install instruction:
1. Install the plugin from this site
2. You will need to install a compatible python version in addition to this.
I recommend downloading this:
https://www.python.org/ftp/python/3.7.3/python-3.7.3-embed-win32.zip 

3. extract the zip somewhere (does not matter where) and add the location to PATH (video)
(The video also shows an optional step of how to add scripts to the plugin)
Video: https://youtu.be/vcavTkvWJvs

4. Restart for the env path variable to update globally 
5. Activate the plugin with the plugin manager
shortDescription: A plugin with bindings between the BakkesmodSDK and python

name: Rocket Recorder
description:
This plugin allows recording your game at 120hz in a similar way as replays do (Currently limited to 1v1).

The replays are saved as flatbuffer binaries.
Flatbuffer schematic: https://pastebin.com/0FqrF729
More info about flatbuffers: https://google.github.io/flatbuffers/

You can also opt-in to automatically upload your data to a server, so everyone has access to more training data!

This plugin is primarily made for machine learning enthusiasts who are interested in collecting training data for their networks.

Join our discord if you need help or have suggestions: https://discord.gg/HhPhVGh
shortDescription: This plugin allows recording your game at 120hz for use in machine learning applications.

name: Creative Mode Plugin
description:
This plugin allows the Creative Mode custom map to save and load levels from you and other people! When you save a level inside the map, a .data file will be created with all the information needed to load the level again.

The plugin has 2 bits of customization for you to use if you so wish. These can be configured from the F2 settings page for the plugin.
- Where the levels are stored. By default this is `{bakkesmod folder}/data/CreativeMode/Maps`. You can change this to any folder you want. This is also where any maps shared with you will need to be placed in order to appear in the map.
- on/off switch for logging information to the console

Have fun creating all sorts of levels!
