name: GameStats
description:
Export basic match statistics for all players to CSV files. Perfect for tournament organizers and league administrators who need to track essential player performance metrics in a simple, accessible format.

## Join our discord
Please consider joining our [discord](https://discord.gg/ycrbhbAKaK) if you have any questions, feedbacks or suggestions, I'd be happy to help!

## **Features**
- Automatically records statistics at the end of each match
- Exports data in CSV format for easy import into spreadsheets
- Avoids losing player's data if they disconnect before the match ends
- Set a maximum amount of saved files (auto delete oldest files)
- Captures essential player metrics including:
  - Score
  - Goals scored
  - Assists made
  - Saves performed
  - Shots taken
  - Demolitions inflicted
  - Amount of boosts/pads picked up
  - Boost usage
  - Possession time

### Limitations
| Statistic Type | As Player | As Spectator | Mid-Game Join Behavior |
|----------------|-----------|--------------|------------------------|
| Score          |  Tracked |  Tracked   |  Full history available |
| Goals          |  Tracked |  Tracked   |  Full history available |
| Assists        |  Tracked |  Tracked   |  Full history available |
| Saves          |  Tracked |  Tracked   |  Full history available |
| Shots          |  Tracked |  Tracked   |  Full history available |
| Demolitions    |  Tracked |  Tracked   |  Only tracked from join time |
| Boosts/Pads Pickup |  Not tracked |  Tracked |  Only tracked from join time |
| Boost Usage    |  Player only |  All players |  Only tracked from join time |
| Possession Time    |  Tracked  |  Tracked  |  Only tracked from join time |

#### Notes:
- If a player joins a match in progress, basic statistics (score, goals, etc.) will include the full match history.
- Boost-related and possession time statistics are only recorded from the moment the user connects to the match.
- In player mode, only the user's boost usage is tracked.
- In spectator mode, boost usage for all players is tracked.

## **Options**
**Toggle auto save stats into a file** - Define if the plugin saves the stats into a .csv file at the end of a match  
**Switch language** - Translate plugin interface (English and French supported)  
**Auto Export** - Automatically export stats at match end without prompting  
**Export Directory** - Choose where CSV files are saved  
**Toggle Limit files saved** - Define if the plugin removes oldest files to avoid too many files  
**Set the max files you want to keep** - Default to 50, you can keep up to 500 stats files. This option defines how many files will be kept (the newest)  

## **CSV Data Fields**
The exported data includes the following fields:
- End match date/time
- Player names
- Team Numbers
- Team Names
- Account IDs
- Platform Name
- Score
- Goals
- Assists
- Saves
- Shots
- Demolitions
- Pads
- Boosts
- BoostUsage
- PossessionTime

## **Commands**
Access the plugin with the following console commands:
- `save_game_stats` - Manually trigger stats export
- `log_game_stats` - Log current stats

## **Update Log**
**v1.0.0**
- Initial release with core functionality
- Support for all game modes (Online)
- Basic statistic export
- Basic statistic preview
- Plugin translation (French and English supported)

**v1.1.0**
- Limit the amount of saved files

**v1.2.0**
- Track of possession time by players
- Track picked up boosts and pads
- Track boost usage

## **Troubleshooting**
If the plugin isn't working as expected:
- Check that the plugin is enabled in the BakkesMod settings  
- Verify if save path folder is correct  
- Check the Limitations section  

## **Feedback and Support**
For bugs, feature requests, or general feedback, please [Join our discord](https://discord.gg/ycrbhbAKaK)
shortDescription: The Match Statistics Exporter plugin for Rocket League automatically records and exports basic player statistics from matches to CSV files. It captures essential data including goals, assists, saves, shots, and demos, boosts/pads picked up, boost usage and possession time for all players in the match. Ideal for tournament organizers and league administrators who need simple player performance tracking for their events.

name: NanoLeaf for Rocket League
description:
# NanoLeaf for Rocket League
BakkesMod Plugin to integrate Rocket League events with NanoLeaf wall-panels. Unfortunately, light bulbs are not supported and do not seem to have the possibility to be supported in the future.

If you *really* want to buy me a coffee: [![ko-fi](https://ko-fi.com/img/githubbutton_sm.svg)](https://ko-fi.com/gtt1229)


## Discord: [Rocket League Assistant](https://discord.gg/qxWh36hY6K)

![rlnanobanner](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cee2e19f-f2d3-4ec8-a1c9-1db895a8eb62)


## Special thanks to those in the Discord and notably [Branky](https://github.com/ItsBranK) and [JerryTheBee](https://github.com/ubelhj)
And some other person named Josh

# NanoLeaf Configuration
The plugin utilizes NanoLeaf's local API and requires an authentication token to be generated.

## NanoLeaf Authentication Token:

   1. Populate your NanoLeaf's IP
   
   2. Hold the on-off button down for 5-7 seconds until the LED starts flashing
   
   3. Click "Generate NanoLeaf Token"

## Plugin Configuration Notes:

* Light colors are not 1:1, so please test colors by pressing the "Test Lights Using FreePlay Color" button in the plugin settings.
* The "Enable (Demos/Goals) Based on Teams' Color" option result in brighter colors of the relevant team that scored or demo'd.
* The "Get Panels" button can retrieve your panelID's but it does not currently do anything.
   
### Notes

* NanoLeaf tokens are secrets and should be treated as such. This plugin stores your token in plain text and makes no attempt to obffuscate, encrypt, or hash your token.

* If you manually reload the plugin (through the F6 BakkesMod Console) while in the game, the IP and Authenticatio noken will have to be reentered.

## To Do

* Possibly single panel configuration
* Probably code clean up and Nullchecks
shortDescription: BakkesMod Plugin to integrate Rocket League events with NanoLeafs

name: Better POV Bindings
description:
This plugin is made for observers and solves the issue of player bindings changing between games.

In Rocket League???s spectator mode, player bindings typically change between games making it difficult or even impossible for observers to switch POVs smoothly, as the keys no longer correspond to the same players and can easily lead to mistakes.

With this plugin, you'll have consistent player bindings across all games. Here are the available commands :

`pov_blue_1` : switch to Team Blue's first player in alphabetical order

`pov_blue_2` : switch to Team Blue's second player in alphabetical order

`pov_blue_3` : switch to Team Blue's third player in alphabetical order

`pov_orange_1` : switch to Team Orange's first player in alphabetical order

`pov_orange_2` : switch to Team Orange's second player in alphabetical order

`pov_orange_3` : switch to Team Orange's third player in alphabetical order

## Credits

Plugin commissioned by [Limbo](https://x.com/Limb0_RL), made by [Vync](https://twitter.com/Vync5).
shortDescription: This plugin ensures consistent spectator POV bindings across games

name: Wait Command
description:
## Usage

`wait`  
`wait_once`

Example:  
`wait 1000 "ball location 0 0 1000"`

## Description

`wait` will wait the specified amount of milliseconds of in-game time before executing the command.  
It therefore works for different game speeds.

`wait_once` overwrites the previous command, if it didn't yet execute.  
`wait` allows multiple commands to be queued.

Example:  
`wait 1000 "ball location 0 0 1000"; wait 2000 "ball stop"` will move the ball after 1 second and freeze it after 2 seconds.  
`wait_once 1000 "ball location 0 0 1000"; wait_once 2000 "ball stop"` will only freeze the ball after 2 seconds.
shortDescription: Execute a command after some in-game time. It's like `sleep` but works for different game speeds!

name: Preset Binder
description:
# Preset Binder
Preset Binder is a plugin used for BakkesMod

### About
This plugin allows you to bind a BM item mod code to a specific car preset, so BM presets are automatically applied once a preset is swapped. Useful for any player that frequently uses BM presets with items they don't have - so almost everyone :).

### Usage
For each preset you have, there will be a drop down bar where you can add the item code you wish to bind to that preset. The bar will highlight blue if there is a binding to it and green if it is currently equipped.

### Update Log
v1.0.0 - Release
v1.0.1 - Fixed bug where all 50 preset slots would appear even if you do not have 50 presets.
v1.1.0 - Removed 'Test' button and added 'Edit' for every binding.
v1.1.1 - Fixed bug causing dll to be installed with a space in the name - resulting in plugin completely not working.
shortDescription: Binds BM item mod codes to in-game car presets.

name: MusicSync
description:
# MusicSync
In-game overlay to display the current media playing using the Windows Media API. **NOT MAC/LINUX COMPATIBLE**

Whatever media is most recently started will show in the in-game overlay. This means, if you are watching a Youtube video, and then playing another Youtube video, the new video will take over.



## Make it your own!



## Compatibilitiy
This is app agnostic, so as long as the application supports the Windows Media API, the overlay will populate. You can see if your media player is supported on Windows 11 by locking your screen and seeing if the media control panel is on:
shortDescription: An in-game overlay for currently playing media

name: AnonymousPfps
description:
Full credit: Martinn
For streamers who dont want nsfw profile pictures uncontrolled by them showing up on the scoreboard

# On/Off guide
As this plugin doesnt have a "On/Off" switch, there is multiple actions you could take to turn "Off" the plugin
by pressing Function key 6 (F6) and typing "plugin unload anonymouspfps", this will unload the plugin *for the rest of the session* and will promptly load the plugin again when rocketleague starts and bakkesmod injects into rocketleague

# Uninstalling
In the main bakkesmod *Injector window*, you should see header buttons ("File", "Links", "Settings", "Help") click "File" and then "Open Bakkesmod Folder", then located in the "Plugins" folder should be the "AnonymousPfps.dll" file. This is the file you want to delete to *fully remove* AnonymousPfps. 

However if you wish to keep it from loading but still keep it around, you can follow the same steps as above but instead go to "cfg"/"plugins.cfg" and remove the line "plugin load anonymouspfps"

If the plugin does stop working do not message me
shortDescription: Removes ingame pfps (Steam Only)

name: SSA Plugin
description:
Automate the creation and management of private Rocket League matches. Pair your game with the Discord bot, generate a private lobby with one click, assign players to voice channels, and record match winners. All seamlessly within your server workflow.

---

## Features
- Pairing with Discord `deviceId`.
- Auto-host private matches (name/password/region) directly from the bot.
- **Connect** button with protection (anti-spam/debounce, no Bakkes crashes).
- Fixed map rotation optimized for training:
  - Mannfield Night  Forbidden Temple  DFH  Utopia (Dusk)  Champions.
- Permissions/organization: split teams into separate voice channels; tag players in text.
- Match result tracking (captain confirms; points update in the server???s leaderboard).

## Commands
- **Menu hotkey (default F4)**:
---

## FAQ
**Does the plugin provide in-game advantages?**  
No. It only automates private lobbies and structured map rotations for training and competition.

**Do I need the Discord bot?**  
For full automation, yes. But the plugin also works locally using the built-in commands above.

---

## Requirements
- Rocket League (Windows)  
- BakkesMod updated

---

Developed by the **SSL Athletes community**! 

Thanks to everyone who helped test and refine the training!

name: Altitude Meter
description:
This BakkesMod plugin displays the real-time altitude of your car and the ball as an overlay in Rocket League, helping you visualize height differences and improve your aerial play.

## Join our discord
Please consider joining our [discord](https://discord.gg/ycrbhbAKaK) if you have any questions, feedbacks or suggestions, I'd be happy to help!

## **Features**

- Real-time altitude gauge displayed on your screen
- Car altitude tracking (blue indicator)
- Ball altitude tracking (red indicator)
- Altitude markers
- Vertical or horizontal orientation options
- Customizable screen positioning
- Adjustable size settings
- Multilingual support (English and French)

## **Options**

**Language** - Choose the plugin language (English or French supported)  
**Enable/Disable Plugin** - Toggle the plugin's functionality on or off  
**Display Ball Altitude** - Show or hide the ball's altitude indicator   
**Ball indicator color**  - Define the ball indicator color  
**Car indicator color**  - Define the car indicator color  
**Display Car Altitude** - Show or hide your car's altitude indicator  
**Car indicator color**  - Define the car indicator color  
**Display Markers** - Show markers  
**Markers color**  - Define the markers color  
**Horizontal Mode** - Switch between vertical and horizontal display orientation  

### Position & Size Settings
- **Horizontal/Vertical Mode** - Define the bar orientation  
- **X/Y Position** - Adjust the gauge's position on your screen
- **Width** - Set the width of the altitude meter
- **Height** - Set the height of the altitude meter
- Quick center buttons for easy positioning


## **Compatibility**

The plugin works in the following modes:
- Freeplay
- Local matches
- Custom Training
- Workshop maps

**Note**: The plugin is automatically disabled during online matches to avoid any conflicts.

## **Feedback and Support**

For bugs, feature requests, or general help:
- Discord: [Join our server](https://discord.gg/ycrbhbAKaK)
- Donate: [PayPal](https://paypal.me/joanbgadev)

name: FixTrainingGameSpeed
description:
# FixTrainingGameSpeed

BM has a problem where it aggressively sets the game speed. This means that in training modes, when you set the game speed through the game's menus, BM will override the setting after certain things like a goal replay. This plugin manages that game speed setting alongside your in-game setting.

## What does this do?
Fixes your game speed setting in BakkesMod while in a training game mode to reflect what you have set in game.  
If you change the training game speed through BM, you'll see it when you go to the settings.  
If you change the training game speed in the game's menu, you'll see it reflected in BM.

---

## ISSUES:
IF YOU HAVE ANY ISSUES, PLEASE REPORT THEM [HERE](https://github.com/mgavin/FixTrainingGameSpeed/issues)
shortDescription: Fixes the issue where bakkesmod ignores the game speed set in game in training modes.

name: Multi-bind
description:
# multi-bind

Be able to bind one action to multiple keys without needing to modify your `TAInput.ini` file.

---
## Motivation

Rocket League doesn't currently allow users to bind more than one button per action within the game.  For example, if you want to bind the action of `Jump` to `Y` **AND** `B`, you just couldn't do in the game's configuration settings. But it's an open "secret" that you *could* do that by editing your `TAInput.ini` file. With this plugin, you will be able to have the ability to do that in game instead.

The problem with editing the `TAInput.ini` file directly is that it gets replaced frequently and that's just frustrating. You may as well set it with a plugin and not worry about it. (Unless you prefer edit other things in there, but that's a separate issue.)

The benefits of doing it this way will let you be able to customize different parts of a bind, while holding onto and saving your settings.  For example, there's a parameter that lets you be able to affect the **strength** of the particular action.

## Is this cheating?

The major point of contention regarding "cheating" in Rocket League is being able to perform macros. **Editing your `TAInput.ini` file (nor the equivalent methods simulated here) in no-way-shape-or-form allow you to perform "macros".** "Macros" are understood to be a set of actions executed in a sequence. When you assign multiple actions to one button, all of the actions are attempted to be done at once.

For example, if you bind `Jump` and `Jump` to the same key, you will perform only one `Jump`. Although, if you bind
`Jump` and `YawLeft` to the same key, you will be able to Jump and turn slightly to the left with one input.

You can already achieve this behavior in the game, by going into your controller configuration and do the equivalent
bind of `Air Steer Left` and `Jump` to the same key. Unfortunately, you're only limited to setting one input per action
from the game's menu, even though the game itself allows you to have multiple bindings per action.

For any sort of discussion on the topic of `TAInput.ini`, search the r/RocketLeague subreddit.

## What's the point of this?

To encourage Psyonix to clarify their position on the subject, or to urge them to add more functionality into the game itself.

If they would open up the controller configuration settings so that multiple bindings can be set per action, that would be one way to do the same thing they enable doing now.

If they would add multiple actions under one bindable setting into the configuration settings, that would be a nice
compromise. For example, you can perform a `Stall` by binding the `YawLeft` (or `YawRight`), the opposite roll direction
`RollRight` (or `RollLeft`) respectively, and `Jump` onto the same input key (TIP: the strength of the binds to a high
value so that your inputs dont possibly affect the yaw or roll inputs!) . Then, after the initial jump, press the bind
to perform the `Stall`. If Psyonix added a `Stall` action to the game, you could bind a button to that instead through
the game's menus.

I want to give people the ability to do what they can now, without having to worry about babysitting an ini file.

## Can you do this on consoles?

I don't know. Sorry console users. :(

## What if a certain thing doesn't work?

I know that doing an action of `MusicNextTrack` works in the main menu, but doesn't work in game.  
I don't know why. Ask Psyonix.

## What if I want to be able to set more than 40 binds?

I don't know if I'll increase that limit. Forty is really pushing the bounds of necessity.

---

## ISSUES:
IF YOU HAVE ANY ISSUES, PLEASE REPORT THEM [HEREEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEE](https://github.com/mgavin/multi-bind/issues)
shortDescription: Be able to bind the same action to multiple keys.

name: AutoForfeit
description:
# AutoForfeit

Have you ever wished you could bypass the mental trauma of having to open the menu, scrolling down to [vote to forfeit],
and hitting confirm, when you immediately felt the desire to?

Well then your prayers have been addressed!

With this plugin you can set it to...  
### Start a forfeit when:
- The in-game clock reaches a certain point
- Your team scores a certain amount of goals
- The opponent team scores a certain amount of goals
- The goal differential between your team and the opponent team is a certain amount
- You're in a specific playlist! (have it work in casual 1s and nowhere else, for example)

And for all those options for when you can start a forfeit... They can also be set to occur in response to !!! **a teammate forfeiting!** !!! Wow!  
And you can **disable it while in a party**! (local party or not)
And you can call (or bind) `aff_forfeit` from the bakkesmod console to forfeit!

That's pretty much it...


![Settings!](https://github.com/mgavin/AutoForfeit/assets/1364492/79260d96-fa1c-4372-b790-13806131e00b)

If you have any issues, [please inform me about them on the issues page!](https://github.com/mgavin/AutoForfeit/issues)
shortDescription: Automatically forfeit during a match given set conditions.

name: Match Admin Hotkeys
description:
I was watching Leth's video where they're playing squash and the only way to keep track of the score seemed really cumbersome, so I wanted to make something that could solve that problem.

This plugin allows you to change the score, pause/unpause the match, and reset to kickoff using bindable hotkeys (on the keyboard).

_Note that this only works in LAN matches. If I can find a way to get it to function in private online matches, I'll update this._


### 1.0.0 features:
* Configurable keybinds that allow you to:
   * Control the score for each team
   * Pause/Unpause the match
   * Reset to kickoff


### Default keybinds:
- Blue +1: `U`
- Blue ???1: `J`
- Orange +1: `I`
- Orange ???1: `K`
- Pause/Unpause: `P`
- Reset to Kickoff: `O`

_I chose these because I wanted to avoid conflicts with keys that are already bound in Rocket League._

_Originally I was going to use number keys, but those are defaulted to quick chat. You can change the above keybinds to anything you want, but just know that if you choose a key that already controls something in the game, it'll perform both actions._

**Note 11/25/25:** I know the default keybinds aren't ideal since it interferes with text chat. A fix for this will be in the next update. Open to thoughts on which keybinds work best.

### Roadmap:

* Update the default keybinds so they don't conflict with text chat.
   * _12/02/25 - I'm working on this and have something that's almost ready to release._
   * _1/05/26 - I may have figured out a workaround for the text chat issue._
* ~Add the ability to change the game time.~
   * _12/02/25 - I tried to add this, but I don't think it's possible._
* Add the ability to bind controller inputs (this one might be weird since there's no way for Bakkesmod to unbind Rocket League's in-game actions, so there would be conflicts).
* Change the ugly default font.

If you have any ideas for other features, shoot me a message.

Discord: eddie2sweaty

name: FURLS
description:
# FURLS 1.0 (Fluxx's Ultimate Rocket League Stats)

A comprehensive BakkesMod plugin that tracks and displays detailed match statistics with a customizable post-game stats window and interactive shot heatmap.

## Features

### **Post-Game Stats Window**
- **Average Speed** - Track your movement speed in KPH and MPH
- **Boost Stats** - Monitor boost collected and used throughout the match
- **Shot Counter** - Count your shots on goal
- **Team Possession** - Track ball possession for your entire team (includes teammates!)
- **Match Duration** - See total game time

### **Shot Heatmap**
- Visual representation of where you take shots from
- 4-tier color gradient (Blue ??? Green ??? Yellow ??? Red)
- Shows shot frequency and patterns
- Helps identify your shooting tendencies

### **Fully Customizable**
- Toggle any stat on/off individually
- Adjustable UI positions for both windows
- Customizable display settings
- Dynamic window sizing based on enabled stats

### **Smart Detection**
- Accurate boost pad detection (distinguishes between small and large pads)
- Position-based large pad detection using field locations
- Team-aware possession tracking
- Automatic match reset and stat tracking


### **Plugin Settings**

Access settings through: **BakkesMod (F2) ??? Plugins Tab ??? FURLS**

#### **Display Toggles**
- ??? Show Speed
- ??? Show Boost
- ??? Show Shots  
- ??? Show Possession
- ??? Show Game Time

#### **Position Settings**
- Heatmap X/Y Position (0-1920 / 0-1080)
- Post-Game Stats X/Y Position (0-1920 / 0-1080)
- Reset to Defaults button

#### **Quick Actions**
- Enable All Stats
- Disable All Stats
- Reset Stats
- Export Heatmap CSV

## Customization

### **Default Positions**
- **Heatmap:** X=1154, Y=60 (top-right area)
- **Post-Game Stats:** X=1441, Y=29 (right side)

### **Creating Your Layout**
1. Open plugin settings in BakkesMod
2. Adjust X/Y sliders to position windows
3. Toggle stats on/off to customize display
4. Changes apply immediately


## Statistics Explained

### **Average Speed**
- Calculated from position changes over time
- Displayed in both KPH and MPH
- Tracks movement throughout active gameplay

### **Boost Collected/Used**
- **Collected:** Total boost picked up from pads
- **Used:** Total boost consumed
- Smart detection distinguishes between 12 boost (small) and 100 boost (large) pads
- Position-aware detection for accurate counting

### **Shots on Goal**
- Counts shots registered by the game
- Uses ball touch position for heatmap accuracy
- Debounced to prevent duplicate counting

### **Team Possession**
- Tracks when ANY teammate is within 300 units of the ball
- Displayed as time and percentage
- More accurate than individual possession tracking

### **Match Duration**
- Total active gameplay time
- Excludes replays and goal celebrations

## Changelog

### **v1.0.0** (Initial Release)
- Post-game stats window with 5 stats
- Shot heatmap with 4-tier gradient
- Customizable stat display
- Adjustable UI positions
- Team possession tracking
- Smart boost pad detection
- Position-based large pad identification
- Automatic match reset
- Goal celebration stat pause

### **v1.0.1** 
- Upgraded shot recognition
- Shot heatmap with 4-tier gradient
- Control panel with custom UI
- Fixed windows not scaling properly depending on resolution

### **v1.0.2** 
- Own goals no longer count as shots
- Added Gaussian Blur to heatmap
- Revamped Control Panel
- Fixed window scaling not going past 2x
- Changed UI of heatmap and stats screen
- Fixed positional settings not saving on restart
- Added second heatmap that displays in main menu (temporary fix)

### **v1.0.3** 
- Added web dashboard with API integration

name: What if...?
description:
# What if... This plugin attemps to answer questions that I had, basically "what if we were able to...". I wanted to make the ability to entertain those questions available to the public to play around with. --- # Disclaimer: It's **IMPERATIVE** that you understand: **THIS WILL ONLY WORK IN OFFLINE GAMES!** **MODIFYING THE GAME TO GAIN AN UNFAIR ADVANTAGE AGAINST SOMEONE WHO DOESN'T HAVE THE ABILITY TO DO THE SAME IS**
CHEATING!
 CHEATING IS A BANNABLE OFFENSE AND WILL NOT BE ENCOURAGED OR TOLERATED. THIS PLUGIN AIMS TO INSPIRE PEOPLE TO SEE THE POTENTIAL IN WHAT PSYONIX COULD INCORPORATE INTO THE GAME AT A GAMEPLAY LEVEL.

IF YOU SUPPORT THE IDEAS IN THIS PLUGIN, PLEASE PETITION PSYONIX TO CONSIDER ADDING THEM TO THE GAME! (through the subreddit or whatever)

Offline games include every game mode that isn't online. So, you can try this out vs bots in a lan match, workshop map, a custom training map, etc. --- Note: You need to re-enter the game mode when you change the settings. --- So far it offers: ## 1. What if we had the ability to have a "Reverse Camera" binding instead of *only* the "Rear View"? Turn your camera around 180?? with the push of a button instead of only being able to look behind your car. You can keep your "Rear View" bind while you try this out, since there is an option to turn off that functionality while the "reverse camera" is enabled. You can invert the swivel setting's inversion. This means that not only will the camera rotate around the left/right direction (Yaw), it will also rotate around the up/down direction (Pitch). ## 2. What if we had a bind to "cancel a flip" during a dodge? At the press of a button, you can make sure to "cancel" the pitch momentum that your car carries through a dodge. The pitch momentum is cancelled *\*completely*\* no matter where you're holding your stick (or mouse or whatever). It will help you imagine what it would look like if you had the ability to *\*perfectly\** cancel your pitch during situations where you dodge, like during a half flip, or during a speed flip, or any other situation. --- Possible changes: - Adding the ability to assign multiple keys to bind (this may come out as a separate plugin) - Other "what if" questions? - I don't have any that I'm currently considering. I have so many other things that I want to work on that I'm not interested in thinking any up. Maybe if I get inspired, I'll change that. If there are any issues with the usage of the plugin, PLEASE! submit them [HERE](https://github.com/mgavin/What-if...-/issues)!
shortDescription: A bakkesmod plugin that asks "what if we were able to do certain things in Rocket League?"

name: Goal Predictor
description:
**Goal Predictor** runs a machine learning model to predict the probability of each team scoring within the next 10 seconds. The predictions are rendered as a real-time scrolling graph overlay, allowing you to visualize which team has the advantage as it shifts. We use a Transformer-based model trained on **RLCS data**.

This plugin currently only works for **3v3 matches** when **spectating** online games or viewing **replays**.

# HOW TO INSTALL

The bakkesplugins.com download only contains the `GoalPredictor.dll` file but you also need two files in the `bakkesmod/data` folder.  See the [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/dster2/rocket-league-goal-predictor/releases) for the full installation.

## Model

Our machine learning model has about 1.8 million parameters and utilizes a custom **Transformer architecture**, similar to the technology powering LLMs. Each prediction is made from one snapshot of in-game data (no past frames). The model represents the game state as a collection of **15 entities**:
*   **1 Ball** (position, velocity)
*   **6 Players** (position, velocity, forward direction, up direction, angular velocity, boost, respawn timer)
*   **6 Big boost orbs** (fixed position, respawn timer)
*   **2 Goals** (fixed position)

and uses a custom attention mechanism with geometric biases to weigh the importance of every entity-to-entity interaction, then aggregates this information into a final probability distribution prediction.

The model does *not* have access to **current score** or **current time** information. This is to focus purely on current game state and avoid a bias of which team is currently winning, but it also means the model has **no understanding of realistic zero-second behavior** where 1) the 10 second time horizon is cut off and 2) each team's gameplay tactics might diverge from normal based on the current score or the need to keep the ball up.

The model also does not have access to whether players have their **flip available**.  This would be very useful information, but unfortunately I don't believe it's possible to accurately determine from replay files nor from the in-game data available to BakkesMod.  Instead the model tries to infer based on other context.

### Training
The model is trained on in-game data from Rocket League Championship Series (RLCS) matches, specifically online matches from North America and Europe in Seasons 2022-23, 2024, and 2025.

## Open-source resources

This project is entirely open-source, from the raw replay files and parsing, to the ML model, to this BakkesMod plugin.

1.  **RLCS replay files:** A collection of Europe, North America, and LAN replay files from RLCS [Season X](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/dster/rocket-league-rlcs-season-x-replays), [21-2022](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/dster/rocket-league-rlcs-season-2021-22-replays), [2022-23](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/dster/rocket-league-rlcs-season-2022-23-replays), [2024](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/dster/rocket-league-rlcs-season-2024-replays), and [2025](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/dster/rocket-league-rlcs-season-2025-replays).
2.  **[Replay parsing library](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/dster/carball-fork):** Custom fork of the abandoned [carball](https://github.com/SaltieRL/carball) project to parse replay files, with various bugfixes and improvements.
3.  **[Timeseries parsing code](https://www.kaggle.com/code/dster/rocket-league-rlcs-timeseries-parsing):** Parses replays and cleans / filters bad data to construct the Timeseries Dataset.
4.  **[Timeseries dataset](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/dster/rocket-league-rlcs-timeseries):** The parsed, cleaned in-game timeseries data from the RLCS replay files.
5.  **[Kaggle Competition](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/rocket-league-rlcs-goal-prediction-2025):** Testbed to train a model and verify its accuracy on the test dataset.
6.  **[Model code](https://kaggle.com/code/dster/rocket-league-goal-predictor-2025-model):** ML model definition, training, and export pipelines.
7.  **[BakkesMod plugin](https://github.com/dster2/rocket-league-goal-predictor):** This plugin which runs the model on in-game data in real-time within Rocket League itself.
shortDescription: Predicts which team will score next in real-time using a machine learning model trained on RLCS matches

name: Revela's Input HUD
description:
I've longed for a plugin that would overlay my in-game inputs and not compromise when it comes to an informative display. 

*Introducing:*  **Revela's Input HUD**
(New update: **v1.2.0** releasing soon)

There's an information-gap that exists while spectating controller overlays. Streamers and YouTubers have their own set of controls that are both varying and visually imprecise when shown via a controller overlay. This makes it difficult for spectators to mentally translate the controller inputs to in-game actions, since everyone likes to use their own blend of control bindings. This plugin aims to bridge this information-gap, visually showing only the relevant in-game actions.

---

### **Features:**

*   **Instant response time:** Ties directly into Bakkesmod's ControllerInput struct (all controllers and mkb compatible).
*   **Left vertical bar:** Accelerate/Reverse (Full spectrum analog).
*   **Top horizontal bar:** Air roll right/left (Full spectrum analog from the bar's midpoint).
*   **Center square:** Left analog stick (Full spectrum analog with vertical/horizontal cross-sectional locator to improve visual-tracking).
*   **SLD:** Power Slide indicator.
*   **JMP:** Jump indicator.
*   **BST:** Boost indicator.

---

### **NEW in Version 1.2.0 (currently *pending review* as of writing this):**

*   **Replay Reconstruction:** Rocket League replays do not save controller data. This plugin uses an advanced **Inverse Physics Engine** to mathematically reconstruct player inputs (Pitch, Yaw, Roll, and Acceleration) in real-time while watching replays. Quite honestly, it's still pretty shabby and has a ton of artifacts... but it works (kinda).
*   **Fully Customizable Layout:** Access the new configuration suite via the **F2 -> Plugins** menu. Adjust the global position and scale of the entire HUD to fit your screen perfectly.
*   **Individual Component Offsets:** Fine-tune your layout with dedicated location "drags" for the **SLD, BST, and JMP** indicators, allowing you to move them anywhere relative to the main HUD.
*   **Preset Management System:** Save and name multiple HUD configurations. Switch between different layouts (e.g., "Stream Mode" vs. "Analysis Mode") instantly.
*   **Smart Input Smoothing:** Adjustable interpolation settings to ensure that reconstructed inputs look fluid and natural, even at the low replay tick rate.
*   **Collision & External Force Filtering:** The reconstruction math filters out massive torque spikes from ball hits and car bumps to prevent "ghost" inputs on the stick display. I'm not sure how well this works, and I definitely think this is somewhat broken, but I tried my best.
*   **Goal Replay Detection:** The HUD automatically hides during goal replays.
shortDescription: Displays your in-game inputs! This plugin intends to resolve the info-gap that exists with tutorials/streamers that use controller overlays, which can confuse viewers that don't know that player's unique bindings.

name: neuRLcar
description:
neuRLcar is a machine learning model trained on over 100,000 SSL+ Rocket League replays in order to determine the likelihood of which team scores next, given any position. Like Stockfish, but for Rocket League.

Only works on replays.

The python part of this project is too big to be hosted on Bakkesmod. See here for download instructions so you can get the full model working:
https://github.com/neurlcar/neurlcar-python/releases/tag/v1.0.0
shortDescription: Generates and displays a positional evaluation over rocket league replays

name: Auto-PlayerPOV (Saved Replays)
description:
# Auto-PlayerPOV (Saved Replays)

Tired of opening a saved replay and manually fishing through the player list and camera settings every time you open a replay? **Auto-PlayerPOV** handles the setup for you. 

The moment you load a saved match replay, the plugin automatically snaps the camera to your car's perspective. No more menu-fiddling, just load in and watch that insane psycho or triple reset exactly how you lived it.

---

### **Quick Start:**
* **One-Time Setup:** Just jump into a single Freeplay session or match. The plugin will automatically "lock in" your profile.
* **Auto-Focus:** From then on, every saved replay you open will start with the camera focused on YOU.
* **The "Clip-Back" Hotkey:** Switched to Fly cam to see a different angle and want to snap back instantly? Set this up:
   1. Open BakkesMod (**F2**) -> **Bindings** tab.
   2. Click **Add** and pick a key (like `P` or `NumPad1`).
   3. Type this command: `replay_focus_me`

---

### **Why use it?**
* **Skip the Setup:** Goes straight to your POV the second the replay starts.
* **Won't Mess With You:** It only triggers once at the start. If you manually change your camera mid-replay, it stays exactly where you put it.
* **Set and Forget:** Remembers who you are even after you restart Rocket League.

---

**Note:** If you change your name or switch accounts, just hit the **"Reset Profile"** button in the plugin settings to lock in your new identity.
shortDescription: This plugin detects when you open a saved replay and automatically switches the camera to your POV.

name: SilentQueue
description:
# Silent Queue

**Silent Queue** is a lightweight BakkesMod plugin designed to make the matchmaking experience more discreet.

This plugin allows players **??? especially streamers ???** to start matchmaking without the usual immediate UI and audio feedback, reducing unwanted attention during live broadcasts.

**Why streamers**

This plugin fights queue sniping attempts by reducing the visible and audible signals that indicate when queues start.

By limiting broadcast timing cues, Silent Queue tries to make it harder for viewers to synchronize their queues with a streamer.
shortDescription: Discreetly start queue in Rocket League with reduced UI and audio feedback, designed for streamers.

name: Performance Log Plus
description:
Performance Log Plus records performance and network statistics during online Rocket League matches and writes them to a CSV file for later analysis.

This plugin is a maintained fork of the original [Performance Log](https://bakkesplugins.com/plugin/115) plugin by DanMB, which has been archived on GitHub and no longer works with current versions of Rocket League and the BakkesMod SDK.

## What it does
* Logs data once per second during an online match
* Writes all data to a CSV file at the end of each match
* Output file location: `%appdata%/bakkesmod/bakkesmod/data/<UserID>-performance.csv`

## Collected data
* Timestamp
* User ID
* Match GUID
* Server IP
* Server Port
* Playlist
* FPS
* Ping
* Latency
* Packets in / out
* Lost packets in / out
* Input buffer and buffer target

## Additional improvements
* Updated to work with modern BakkesMod versions
* Extended network and performance metrics
* Server IP and port are captured from Rocket League log files
* Collect performance data during entire match instead of only active parts (i.e. where players can move) of the match

All credit for the original concept goes to DanMB. Special thanks to Harry (Hebbins).
shortDescription: Maintained and extended Performance Log plugin with additional network metrics.
